general_exclusion_criteria:
* Active or progressive central nervous system involvement (brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease).
* Persistently elevated (>Grade 1) toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia and vitiligo).
* Recent extensive radiotherapy (wide‑field >30% marrow‑bearing bone) or focal palliation near target lesions.
* Major surgery (aside from catheter/tumor biopsy insertion) lacking sufficient postoperative recovery.
* Clinically significant underlying lung disease requiring systemic steroids within the past six months (interstitial/pneumonia/fibrosis/radiation pneumonitis).
* Current acute/chronic pancreatitis or cirrhosis (unless compensated Child‑Pugh A).
* Clinical hepatic decompensation (jaundice, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding) within 60 days.
* History of liver transplantation.
* Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
* Uncontrolled cardiovascular illness (recent MI/ACS, NYHA Class III/IV CHF, uncontrolled HTN, malignant arrhythmia, severe aortic stenosis).
* Thromboembolic or cerebrovascular events (TIA/CVA/DVT/PE) within three months.
* Active untreated infectious disease—including acute bacterial, fungal, viral infections, hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV (patients with controlled chronic HBV/HCV/HIV may enroll per investigator discretion).
* Known hypersensitivity to the investigational product or excipients.
* Participation in another interventional clinical trial concurrently.
* Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
* Prior invasive malignancy (other than current indication) within three years, except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, non‑muscle‑invasive bladder cancer, in‑situ cervix/prostate/breast cancer with no activity for ≥2 years.

Concurrent receipt of any other investigational agents.

Active uncontrolled hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV infection not adequately managed.

Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Class II‑IV) or serious cardiac arrhythmias; Myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months; Clinically important ECG abnormalities (e.g., left/right bundle‐branch blocks, AV block, prolonged PR interval); Baseline QTcF >470 ms; Inability or refusal to discontinue concomitant QT‑prolonging drugs; History of interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids, or current unexplained ILD/pneumonitis; Significant existing pulmonary disorders (recent PE, severe asthma/COPD, restrictive disease, substantial pleural effusions, autoimmune/inflammatory lung disease, prior total pneumonectomy); Uncontrolled infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy; Clinically significant corneal disease; Known HIV infection; Active viral hepatitis (subject to stipulated virologic thresholds); Pregnancy or nursing motherhood; Untreated/symptomatic active brain metastases (asymptomatic/stable lesions permissible); Unresolved toxicities from preceding anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (chronic grade 2 toxicities allowable upon justification); More than one primary malignancy diagnosed within three years (exemptions for appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ carcinomas, superficially treated urothelial cancer, or contralateral breast cancer); Substance/alcohol dependence posing safety concerns; Hypersensitivity to investigational product components; Any condition deemed by the investigator to render the participant unsafe or unsuitable for study participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: conventional chondrosarcoma. Histology allowed: grade 1 **or** grade 2. Prior treatment required: zero to one prior systemic antitumour regimen in the advanced/metastatic setting (previous radiation acceptable if measurable disease lies outside the irradiated field or shows ≥20 % growth since irradiation). Prior treatment excluded: any prior therapy with an IDH1 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: central‑lab confirmation of an IDH1 R132C/L/G/H/S mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Unable to swallow oral medication.; Pregnancy or breastfeeding.; Participation in another interventional study concurrently.; Receipt of systemic anticancer therapy within 2 weeks of randomisation (investigational or immune‑based agents within 4 weeks) – washout periods omitted here.; Receive radiotherapy within 2 weeks of randomisation – washout omitted.; Symptomatic brain metastases requiring >10 mg/day prednisolone equivalents (stable untreated brain lesions allowable under stipulated conditions).; History of another primary malignancy except cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, cured carcinoma in situ, pT1‑2 prostate cancer Gleason < 6, or disease‑free ≥1 year.; Major surgery within 4 weeks preceding randomisation.; Significant active cardiovascular disease within 6 months (New York Heart Association class III/IV CHF, MI, unstable angina, stroke).; Left ventricular ejection fraction < 40 %.; Baseline QTcF ≥ 450 ms or other risks for QT prolongation.; Known history of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active central nervous system metastases.
New York Heart Association Class II–IV heart disease, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension, or congestive heart failure.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or hepatitis B/C infection when receiving prohibited concomitant medications.
Major surgical procedures (e.g., abdominal laparotomy) within four weeks preceding first dose.
Inability to ingest oral medication due to gastrointestinal disorders, malabsorption syndrome, or dependence on parenteral nutrition.
Active bleeding events excluding minor hemorrhage from hemorrhoids or gums.

general_exclusion_criteria: Systemic therapeutic radiopharmaceutical administered within six months prior to enrollment. Inability or contraindication to undergo required imaging procedures. Presence of uncontrolled brain metastases requiring steroid, surgical, or radiation intervention. History of an additional malignancy that is progressive or has required active treatment within the preceding three years (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas or carcinoma in situ). Persistent adverse events graded ≥ 2 attributable to prior therapy (with the exception of grade 2 alopecia). Previous organ transplantation, including hematopoietic stem‐cell transplantation. Prior exposure to nitrosoureas or actinomycin‑D. Clinically relevant proteinuria. Documented hypersensitivity to the investigative product or any excipient thereof. Active uncontrolled medical conditions such as serious infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmias, poorly controlled diabetes, or severe psychiatric disorders limiting protocol adherence. Receipt of more than twenty gray total body irradiation to extensive portions of bone marrow.

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with known allergy or hypersensitivity to ivosidenib or venetoclax.; Concurrent uncontrolled medically serious condition (infection, lab abnormality, psychiatric illness).; Prohibited use of other chemotherapeutic or anti‑leukemic agents during study (exceptions: intrathecal chemotherapy for prophylaxis/control of CNS leukemia, hydroxyurea, or a single dose of cytarabine up to 2 g/m^2 for rapid proliferation disease before start and during first four weeks).; Concomitant strong CYP3A inducers (e.g., avasimibe, carbamazepine, phenytoin, rifampin, rifabutin, St. John’s Wort) within three days of starting study medication.; Active graft‑versus‑host disease post‑stem cell transplantation (unless inactive/chronic suppressed immunosuppression discussed with investigator).; Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorder interfering with oral drug absorption.; Ongoing actively treated secondary malignant disease.; Baseline corrected QTc > 450 msec (Fridericia) (with allowance after discussion).; Positive testing for active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus.; White blood cell count exceeding 25 × 10^9/L (Hydroxyurea permitted to reduce).; Pregnancy-positive nursing woman, pregnant woman, or woman of child‑bearing potential unwilling/unable to practice adequate contraception.

Prior treatment required: radical prostatectomy with documented bilateral nerve‐sparing (intracapsular or intermural technique). Prior treatment excluded: prior local prostate cancer therapy such as cryotherapy or brachytherapy; prior post‑prostatectomy or pelvic radiation therapy. Biomarkers required: serum prostate specific antigen ≥0.1 ng/mL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known allergy to hyaluronic acid.
Pathologic T4 disease.
Planned elective pelvic lymph node radiation therapy.
Prior anorectal surgery (low anterior resection, abdominoperineal resection, absent rectum).
Inflammatory bowel disease requiring systemic corticosteroid therapy.
Active connective tissue disorder including lupus or scleroderma.
Any urogenital abnormality that limits access to the Barrigel injection site.
White blood cell count <4000/μL or >12,000/μL.
Hemoglobin <10 g/dL.
Active bleeding disorder or clinically significant coagulopathy (PTT >35 seconds, INR >1.4, platelet count <100,000/mm³).
Serum AST or ALT greater than 2.5 × institutional upper limit of normal.
Creatinine >2.0 mg/dL.
Bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL.
History of chronic renal failure.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL).
Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (history of AIDS). Controlled HIV infection permitted if CD4⁺ ≥350 cells/µL and no AIDS‑defining illnesses.
Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging or PSMA/PET scanning (e.g., non‑MRI‑compatible devices).
Presence of bilateral hip implants unsuitable for MRI (unilateral MRI‑compatible implant may be allowable).
Subject unable or unwilling to comply with study requirements.
Any condition deemed by the investigator to impede administration or completion of study therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma of the prostate. Prior treatment excluded: any 225Ac‑based investigational compound. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positive disease verified by PSMA PET/CT as specified in protocol **and** serum testosterone <50 ng/dL (<1.7 nmol/L). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Investigational agents administered within 28 days prior to randomization.; Central nervous system metastases that are neurologically unstable, symptomatic, or necessitating steroid support.; Acute kidney injury developing 48 hours to 7 days earlier OR chronic kidney disease persisting ≥3 months.; Baseline xerostomia graded ≥2 (CTCAE version 5).; Uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, or coronary artery bypass graft performed within 6 months prior to signing informed consent or presently active severe cardiac disease.; History of lymphoproliferative disorder or any other malignancy diagnosed within the last 5 years, except satisfactorily treated basal cell carcinoma, actinic keratosis, or non‑invasive colonic polyps removed with no recurrence.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* History of bilateral Wilms tumor (synchronous or metachronous)
* Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection
* Symptomatic congestive heart failure (grade 2 or higher)
* Renal tubular acidosis (serum bicarbonate <16 mmol/L and serum phosphate ≤2 mg/dL or ≤0.8 mmol/L without supplementation) – applied to high‑risk and very high‑risk relapsed FHWT
* Chronic inflammatory bowel disease and/or intestinal obstruction (applicable to stages 2‑4 DAWT and standard‑risk relapsed FHWT)
* Concurrent use of St. John's wort that cannot be discontinued prior to trial commencement
* Pregnant women
* Breastfeeding mothers planning to nurse infant
* sexually active individuals of reproductive potential unwilling to employ effective contraception throughout study participation

general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or lactating individuals; persons unable to use effective contraception; HIV‑positive participants; active untreated hepatitis B or C infection; unresolved acute or chronic infections; uncontrolled congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%; New York Heart Association functional classification worse than class IIb; severe interstitial lung disease or non‑infectious pneumonitis; solid organ transplantation recipient; active second malignant disease requiring treatment or progression within 2 years (excluding adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, prostate intraepithelial neoplasia, or other indolent malignancies); ongoing systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents for ≥4 weeks; recent (<30 days) administration of a live attenuated vaccine; history of severe hypersensitivity reaction to components resembling N‑803 or assay reagents; prior grade 2 or greater hemolytic anemia; autoimmune disorders causing symptomatic disease (including inflammatory bowel disease, rheumatoid arthritis, systemic scleroderma, systemic lupus erythematosus, Wegener’s granulomatosis, Guillain–Barré syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis) – exceptions apply for controlled hypothyroidism (Hashimoto).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent use of any other investigational or study agents intended to treat the underlying malignancy.
Any prior treatment (including ADC) containing a chemotherapeutic agent targeting topoisomerase I, except as stipulated for the specific ADC2 cohorts.
Receipt of live, attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first study dosing.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to trastuzumab deruxtecamide, datopotamab deruxtecan, their excipients, or other monoclonal antibodies.
Major surgery within 2 weeks prior to study treatment initiation.
Uncontrolled intercurrent serious illness—including active infection, uncontrolled non‑malignant systemic disease, uncontrolled seizures, or psychiatric/social situations limiting compliance.
History of or currently active non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or unresolved pneumonitis/ILD at screening.
Significant lung comorbidity (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive disease, substantial pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with pulmonary involvement, or prior complete pneumonectomy).
Corrected QT interval > 470 ms in females or > 450 ms in males.
Cardiac events within six months: coronary revascularisation, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, or cerebrovascular accident/stroke.
Active second malignant neoplasm, except individuals disease‑free ≥ 3 years or with adequately treated in‑situ carcinomas, superficial bladder cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or cervix/prostate carcinoma in situ.
Known untreated or poorly controlled HIV infection.
Active or uncontrolled Hepatitis B or C infection.
Active infectious processes including tuberculosis.
Conditions, therapies, or laboratory abnormalities that could confound study outcomes or impede completion of the trial.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant.

Symptomatic spinal cord compression or imminent cord compromise.

Serious or uncontrolled comorbid conditions (e.g., interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnea requiring supplemental O₂, severe renal insufficiency [creatinine clearance <30 mL/min], significant gastrointestinal inflammatory diseases causing baseline diarrhoea ≥Grade 2).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: KMT2A‑rearranged **or** NUP98‑rearranged **or** NPM1‑mutated. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed disease (single bone marrow specimen demonstrating ≥5 % leukemic blasts by flow cytometry, FISH, or molecular methods, or combined bone‐marrow plus extramedullary/CNS disease meeting the same threshold) **or** refractory disease (bone marrow containing ≥1 % leukemic blasts by multi‑parameter flow cytometry after two cycles of induction therapy). Prior treatment required: prior standard intensive chemotherapy (including induction±consolidation) with recovery from associated toxicities; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation permissible if ≥84 days have passed. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: confirmatory central laboratory detection of the qualifying KMT2A rearrangement, NUP98 rearrangement, or NPM1 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unlikely to comply with study requirements; individuals with Down syndrome; anyone with isolated extramedullary disease; isolated central nervous system relapse or symptomatic CNS3 disease; acute promyelocytic leukemia; juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia; malabsorptive disorders prohibiting oral medication intake; current use of proton pump inhibitors; concomitant participation in another investigational drug study; known inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes; known allergy to any protocol medicines; active, uncontrolled infection; untreated HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection; women of childbearing potential with a positive pregnancy test or breastfeeding; pre‑existing conditions markedly increasing risk of severe infection (e.g., cystic fibrosis, immune deficiencies, chronic bleeding diatheses, unexplained cytopenias); ongoing use of other investigational compounds within 30 days of study initiation; clinically significant congenital cardiovascular abnormalities (including long QT syndrome or major unrepaired structural defects); any underlying illness judged by the principal investigator to render study treatment unsafe or confound safety assessments; hypersensitivity to fludarabine or cytarabine components; receipt of live vaccination within the preceding six months.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: acute lymphoblastic leukemia, mixed‑phenotype acute leukemia, or acute undifferentiated leukemia. Histology allowed: KMT2A‑rearranged **or** NUP98‑rearranged **or** NPM1‑mutated. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed disease (single bone marrow specimen demonstrating ≥5 % leukemic blasts by flow cytometry, FISH, or molecular methods, or combined bone‑marrow plus extramedullary/CNS disease meeting the same threshold) **or** refractory disease (bone marrow containing ≥1 % leukemic blasts by multi‑parameter flow cytometry at the end of induction/consolidation, or persistent measurable residual disease >0.01 % prior to hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; alternatively peripheral blood absolute blast count ≥1,000 cells/µL when marrow cannot be evaluated). Prior treatment required: prior standard intensive chemotherapy (including induction±consolidation) with recovery from associated toxicities; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation permissible if ≥84 days have passed. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: confirmatory central laboratory detection of the qualifying KMT2A rearrangement, NUP98 rearrangement, or NPM1 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unlikely to comply with study requirements; individuals with Down syndrome; anyone with isolated extramedullary disease; isolated central nervous system relapse or symptomatic CNS3 disease; acute promyelocytic leukemia; juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia; malabsorptive disorders prohibiting oral medication intake; current use of proton pump inhibitors; concomitant participation in another investigational drug study; known inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes; known allergy to any protocol medicines; active, uncontrolled infection; untreated HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection; women of childbearing potential with a positive pregnancy test or breastfeeding; pre‑existing conditions markedly increasing risk of severe infection (e.g., cystic fibrosis, immune deficiencies, chronic bleeding diatheses, unexplained cytopenias); ongoing use of other investigational compounds within 30 days of study initiation; clinically significant congenital cardiovascular abnormalities (including long QT syndrome or major unrepaired structural defects); any underlying illness judged by the principal investigator to render study treatment unsafe or confound safety assessments; hypersensitivity to fludarabine or cytarabine components; receipt of live vaccination within the preceding six months.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both females and males. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: at least one active and measurable brain metastasis (longest axial dimension ≥10 mm by CT/MRI) regardless of extracranial disease. Prior treatment required: at least one endocrine therapy administered in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: abemaciclib in the metastatic setting or elacestrant or any other investigative selective estrogen receptor degrader/modulator/ceran/protac agent in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive (≥1 % tumor cells stain positive by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2 negative (IHC score 0–1+ or in situ hybridisation negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: Immediate central nervous system‐specific treatment anticipated; Imminent organ failure or visceral crisis; Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; Advanced/metastatic breast cancer treatment‑naïve status; Concurrent malignant neoplasm or malignancy within past three years (except appropriately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, or second primary breast cancer); Participation in another therapeutic breast cancer trial; Significant unresolved toxicities greater than grade 1 (grade ≤2 acceptable for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); Unstable neurologic symptoms related to brain metastases (<2 weeks stabilization); Concomitant systemic steroids exceeding 4 mg dexamethasone daily or unstable dosage; Severe comorbid conditions preventing safe oral intake or causing malabsorption; Recent major surgery (within four weeks); Clinically meaningful cardiovascular disease (heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmias); Pulmonary toxicity/pneumonitis history; Hepatic impairment beyond stipulated limits; Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <50 mL/min); Ongoing untreated/uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, viral infections—including active hepatitis B or C viremia or uncontrolled HIV infection; Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception measures for individuals of reproductive potential; Strong or moderate CYP3A4 enzyme inducer or inhibitor usage; Certain herbal supplements (kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbine, saw palmetto, ginseng) taken recently; Recent vaccination (including COVID‑19 vaccine) within seven days preceding randomization.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both females and males. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment required: at least one endocrine therapy administered in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive (≥1 % tumor cells stain positive by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2 negative (IHC score 0–1+ or in situ hybridisation negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: Immediate central nervous system‐specific treatment anticipated; Imminent organ failure or visceral crisis; Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; Advanced/metastatic breast cancer treatment‑naïve status; Concurrent malignant neoplasm or malignancy within past three years (except appropriately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, or second primary breast cancer); Participation in another therapeutic breast cancer trial; Significant unresolved toxicities greater than grade 1 (grade ≤2 acceptable for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); Unstable neurologic symptoms related to brain metastases (<2 weeks stabilization); Concomitant systemic steroids exceeding 4 mg dexamethasone daily or unstable dosage; Severe comorbid conditions preventing safe oral intake or causing malabsorption; Recent major surgery (within four weeks); Clinically meaningful cardiovascular disease (heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmias); Pulmonary toxicity/pneumonitis history; Hepatic impairment beyond stipulated limits; Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <50 mL/min); Ongoing untreated/uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, viral infections—including active hepatitis B or C viremia or uncontrolled HIV infection; Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception measures for individuals of reproductive potential; Strong or moderate CYP3A4 enzyme inducer or inhibitor usage; Certain herbal supplements (kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbine, saw palmetto, ginseng) taken recently; Recent vaccination (including COVID‑19 vaccine) within seven days preceding randomization.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: Grade IV glioblastoma, inclusive of gliosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease without prior systemic or radiation therapy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior carmustine wafer implantation or prior intracerebral, intratumoral, or cerebrospinal‐fluid administered agent or prior radiation therapy for glioma or prior chemotherapy or immunotherapy for glioma or receipt of any concurrent active therapy for glioma outside the trial. Biomarkers required: IDH wild‑type (confirmed by immunohistochemistry or DNA sequencing). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Extensive leptomeningeal disease; Corrected QT interval > 450 ms in males or > 470 ms in females; History of another malignant neoplasm within the prior two years (excluding carcinoma in situ or basal / squamous cell skin cancer).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: Grade IV glioblastoma, inclusive of gliosarcoma. Prior treatment required: radiation therapy (received as part of prior standard management). Prior treatment excluded: more than two prior lines of chemotherapy or prior lomustine or prior agents constituting any experimental arm regimen or prior bevacizumab or other VEGF/VEGFR‑directed therapeutic agent or prior carmustine wafer implantation or prior intracerebral drug delivery or receipt of any concurrent active therapy for glioma outside the trial. Biomarkers required: IDH wild‑type (confirmed by immunohistochemistry or DNA sequencing). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Extensive leptomeningeal disease; Corrected QT interval > 450 ms in males or > 470 ms in females; History of another malignant neoplasm within the prior two years (excluding carcinoma in situ or basal / squamous cell skin cancer).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD123 (IL3RA) expression positive on leukemic blasts measured locally within 3 months preceding first protocol treatment. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of acute promyelocytic leukemia; active central nervous system involvement by disease; known positive human immunodeficiency virus infection; chronic active hepatitis B or C infection; clinically significant cardiovascular disease including New York Heart Association Class III–IV heart failure, unstable angina, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmia, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within six months, or corrected QT interval > 480 ms; uncontrolled pulmonary disease; presence of an active advanced solid tumor (other than basal/squamous skin carcinoma or carcinoma in situ); simultaneous hematologic malignancy requiring systemic therapy; current pregnancy or lactation; uncontrolled infectious process; severe gastrointestinal disorders precluding oral intake or absorption; ongoing use of strong cytochrome P450 3A enzyme inducers.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant cardiopulmonary disease (unstable coronary artery disease, recent myocardial infarction, congestive heart failure NYHA class III-IV, arrhythmias requiring medication, uncontrolled hypertension).
Uncontrolled infections requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Active secondary malignant neoplasm within the past three years (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia grade II/III, carcinoma in situ, superficial gastrointestinal tumors, and non‑muscle‑invasive bladder cancer fully resected).
Severe chronic respiratory diseases, active autoimmune/connective tissue disease involving lungs, prior pneumonectomy, or ongoing requirement for supplemental oxygen.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Head and neck cancer (patients who have completed curative intent beam radiation therapy). Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: No evidence of active or recurrent disease; minimum interval of 3 years since completing radiation; presence of grade 2 or grade 3 radiation‑induced late xerostomia. Prior treatment required: Curative beam radiation therapy to the head and neck administered ≥3 years before screening. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Negative Epstein‑Barr virus anti‑viral capsid antigen IgM. Biomarkers excluded: Positive hepatitis C virus infection; positive human immunodeficiency virus infection; active systemic autoimmune disease affecting the salivary glands (e.g., Sjögren’s syndrome); personal or familial predisposition to acute or chronic angle‑closure glaucoma; documented allergy or hypersensitivity to glycopyrrolate.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of recurrent head and neck cancer, parotid gland cancer, or a second primary solid tumour (except treated cutaneous basal/squamous cell carcinoma or in‑situ cervical carcinoma).
Use of systemic immunosuppressive agents (e.g., oral steroids, biologics) within four weeks of enrollment.
Active viral infection with Epstein‑Barr virus confirmed by positive anti‑VCA IgM (unless subsequently rescored as resolved).
Diagnosis of myasthenia gravis.
Current smoker or former smoker who quit ≤3 years ago (including vape/tobacco product use).
Alcohol misuse or consumption exceeding guideline limits within the past 3 years (men >14 drinks/week, women >7 drinks/week in North America; >14 units/week in the United Kingdom).
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA₁c > 7%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: all histologies. Cancer burden allowed: measurable disease per RECIST 1.1 (local, regional, or distant recurrence permitted). Prior treatment required: (at least one prior systemic chemotherapy regimen **AND** prior PD‑1/PD‑L1–directed immunotherapy). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: ARID1A loss‑of‑function mutation confirmed by a CLIA‑certified next‑generation sequencing assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of severe hypersensitivity to avelumab, M1774, or related compounds; ongoing or uncontrolled infection; active autoimmune disease needing systemic immunosuppression; uncontrolled or symptomatic brain metastases or meningeal carcinomatosis; recent major organ/tissue transplantation; significant cardiovascular disorders (uncontrolled hypertension, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, unstable angina, recent MI, serious arrhythmias, cerebrovascular accident/TIA within 180 days); severe gastrointestinal pathology (bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhoea, inflammatory bowel disease flare); psychiatric illness compromising compliance; current pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for women of childbearing potential; QTc interval > 470 msec persisting despite correction; use of contraindicated concomitant medicines (strong CYP3A4/CYP1A2 modulators, PPIs, prohibited herbs, etc.).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Bladder urothelial carcinoma, Renal pelvic urothelial carcinoma, Ureteral urothelial carcinoma, Urethral urothelial carcinoma. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial (>50%), squamous differentiation permissible, small‑cell carcinoma prohibited. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Received prior platinum‑based chemotherapy AND received a checkpoint inhibitor (PD‑1 or PD‑L1) in the locally advanced or metastatic setting (or received checkpoint inhibitor in the peri‑adic setting with relapse within 12 months). Prior treatment excluded: Previously received topoisomerase Ⅰ inhibitors, sacituzumab govitecan, enfortumab vedotin. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or active pulmonary fibrotic disorders. Active or symptomatic cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, significant arrhythmias, EF <40%). Significant hepatic impairment (AST/ALT >5× ULN except with liver mets and albumin >3 g/dL). Severe renal impairment (GFR <30 ml/min/1.73 m²). Untreated or active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Ongoing severe infections or untreated HIV/HBV/HCV infection. Poor performance status (ECOG >1). Concurrent use of high-dose systemic steroids (>10 mg prednisone daily) or other potent immunosuppression. Autoimmune diseases requiring systemic therapy within the past 2 years. Pregnancy or lactation. Any other condition judged by investigator to interfere with safe conduct of the study.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Bladder urothelial carcinoma, Renal pelvic urothelium carcinoma, Ureteral urothelial carcinoma, Urethral urothelial carcinoma. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial (>50%), squamous differentiation permissible, small‑cell carcinoma prohibited. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally advanced unresectable disease. general_exclusion_criteria:
- Pregnant or nursing women.
- Positive serum or urine β‑hCG test indicating pregnancy.
- Requirement for effective contraception throughout the study period for females of reproductive potential.
- Ongoing severe adverse effects from prior anticancer therapy that have not resolved.
- Clinically significant untreated infections, active HIV, chronic Hepatitis B or C infection.
- Severe hepatic impairment (Child–Pugh Class C) or bilirubin elevation greater than institutional limits.
- Moderate-to-severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance less than institution-specified threshold).
- Uncontrolled hypertension or symptomatic congestive heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction below acceptable level.
- Recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, arrhythmia, or other serious cardiac conditions.
- History of arterial or venous thrombosis or pulmonary embolism within the past six months.
- Current interstitial lung disease, radiation recall, or grade >1 pneumonitis/pulmonary fibrosis.
- Brain metastasis that is symptomatic or requires steroids exceeding physiologic doses.
- Prior or concomitant malignancy (other than adequately treated carcinoma in situ or basal/squamous skin cancer) diagnosed within the preceding two years.
- Planned major surgical procedure or trauma expected within four weeks of study entry.
- Conditions impairing gastrointestinal absorption sufficient to compromise oral dosing (malabsorptive syndromes, extensive bowel resection, inflammatory bowel disease flare).
- Known hypersensitivity to any excipient or constituent of DCC‑3084 formulation.

Participation in another investigational drug/device study within 4 weeks prior to enrolment.; Receiving a live attenuated vaccine within 30 days before first dose (killed vaccines allowed).; Concurrent active malignancy that requires treatment or is progressing within the past 3 years (exceptions: cured cutaneous carcinomas, indolent prostate cancer stage T1‑T2a, Gleason ≤6, PSA < 10 ng/ml).; Use of prohibited concomitant medicines that strongly/moderately inhibit or induce CYP3A4/5 or P‑glycoprotein.; Uncontrolled comorbid medical conditions—including active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmias, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (≤6 months), poorly controlled diabetes, recent peptic ulcer disease (≤6 months), clinically significant chronic liver or kidney disease, severe malnutrition.; Significant psychiatric illness or psychosocial situations limiting compliance.; Chronic gastrointestinal disorders, extensive bowel resections, or refractory nausea/vomiting compromising oral drug absorption.; Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) ≥470 ms, congenital long QT syndrome, torsades de pointes susceptibility, or use of medications known to markedly prolong QT interval.; Active tuberculosis or other infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.; Autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within the past two years (aside from physiological hormonal replacements).; Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid therapy >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressants within 7 days of first dose.; Failure to recover fully from major surgery or persisting postoperative complications.; Any condition, therapy, or laboratory abnormality judged by investigators to confound study outcomes or jeopardize participant safety.; Active substance misuse or dependency impacting adherence.; Pregnant or lactating individuals, or anyone planning conception (patient or partner) during study conduct and for stipulated post‑study intervals.; Recipients of allogeneic solid organ or stem cell transplants.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of grade ≥2 pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (heart failure NYHA class III-IV).
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× ULN or AST/ALT > 2.5× ULN).
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Active untreated central nervous system metastases.
Known active infections with HIV (CD4⁺ <250 cells/µL) or uncontrolled hepatitis B/C.
Concurrent severe autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression.
Pregnancy or lactation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All genders. Cancer type allowed: Intracranial diffuse glioma. Histology allowed: Diffuse astrocytoma **or** oligodendroglioma **or** glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: First radiographic relapse after standard therapy; measurable contrast‑enhancing disease (lesion ≥10 mm minimum diameter) or residual disease after salvage surgery (surgery ≥2 weeks ago, clinical status unchanged). Prior treatment required: Prior external beam radiotherapy with concurrent temozolomide followed by up to twelve (≤12) total cycles of adjuvant temozolomide. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.  
Concurrent use of other anticancer agents or devices, including carmustine wafer implants and proteasome inhibitors at recurrence.  
Prior treatment with procarbazine, lomustine, vincristine (PCV) regimen, individual PCV components, carmustine wafer implants, or bevacizumab.  
Prior treatment with poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitors.  
Major surgery (other than surgery for recurrent diffuse glioma) within four weeks prior to study initiation.  
Standard radiotherapy delivered within twelve weeks prior to documentation of progression unless progression lies entirely outside the prior radiation field or is histologically confirmed.  
Prior cranial radiotherapy doses greater than sixty‑five Gray, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy unless recurrence is histopathologically proven.  
Requirement for full‑dose anticoagulation without therapeutic INR/aPTT control and stability for at least two weeks.  
Concomitant medicines that are critical substrates of CYP2D6 or CYP2C19 that cannot be substituted.  
Ongoing use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsant drugs (patients must switch to non‑enzyme inducing alternatives ≥2 weeks before enrollment).  
Pregnancy or lactation.  
Known hypersensitivity to any component of NMS‑03305293 or temozolomide formulation.  
Active infectious diseases (bacterial, fungal, viral—including HIV) necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.  
Baseline corrected QT interval ≥460 ms (women) or ≥450 ms (men) or presence of risk factors for torsades de pointes.  
Clinically significant gastrointestinal conditions impairing oral drug absorption (ulcer disease, inflammatory bowel disease, short‑gut syndrome, etc.).  
Cardiac or vascular event within six months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass/graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure, cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or active bleeding diathesis).  
History of another invasive malignancy within the past three years, excluding non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or successfully treated localized cancer.  
Other serious acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged by the investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound outcome assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All genders. Cancer type allowed: Intracranial glioblastoma, IDH‑wildtype (World Health Organization grade 4). Histology allowed: Glioblastoma **or** diffuse astrocytic glioma, IDH‑wildtype, exhibiting microvascular proliferation **or** necrosis **or** TERT promoter mutation **or** epidermal growth factor receptor amplification **or** combined chromosome 7 gain/chromosome 10 loss (**or**) satisfying c‑IMPACT‑NOW 3 molecular criteria. Cancer burden allowed: First radiographic relapse after completion of standard chemoradiation; measurable contrast‑enhancing disease (lesion ≥10 mm minimum diameter) or residual disease after salvage surgery (required measurable residual, surgery ≥2 weeks ago, clinical status unchanged). Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive radiotherapy with concurrent temozolomide and subsequent adjuvant temozolomide totaling six (≤6) cycles maximum. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: IDH‑wildtype status demonstrated by local immunohistochemistry (reflex DNA sequencing or PCR validation if IHC negative and patient < 55 y). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 2 to 39 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: diffuse midline glioma (including diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma and spinal cord glioma). Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, no prior radiation therapy, lesion located in the pons or spinal cord (thalamic and cerebellar lesions excluded). Prior treatment required: NA. general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic reaction to components of study drugs; current receipt of another investigational anticancer agent; autoimmune diseases requiring systemic cytotoxic/immunosuppressive therapy; known HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C; uncontrolled systemic infection; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraceptive measures for women of child‑bearing potential and men; recent (<24 hours) use of potent CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or indusers (enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants); significant cardiac impairment (ejection fraction ≤45%, symptomatic CHF, prolonged QTc ≥470 ms); hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >3× ULN); renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <70 mL/min/1.73 m²); unresolved grade ≥2 diarrhea; oxygen saturation ≤92% on room air; inability to ambulate sufficiently for performance scoring; existing secondary malignancy requiring active treatment within past 3 years; extensive lepto­meningeal spread or cerebrospinal fluid seeding (unless enrolment in Cohort 5).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell malignant neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after having received at least two prior lines of therapy. Prior treatment required: receipt of a proteasome inhibitor **and** an immunomodulatory imide drug **and** an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody (within ≥2 prior lines of therapy). Prior treatment excluded: any prior B‑cell maturation antigen‑targeted therapy. Biomarkers required: serum M‑protein ≥0.5 g/dL **or** urine M‑protein ≥200 mg/24 h **or** involved serum free light chain ≥100 mg/L together with an abnormal serum κ/λ ratio. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: 12 years and above. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow infiltration with ≥5% blasts; disease must be relapsed or refractory following ≥2 prior systemic antileukemic therapies (or after 1 prior line when no standard‑of‑care alternative exists); no evidence of active central nervous system disease, no isolated extramedullary disease recurrence, and no testicular involvement. Prior treatment required: ≥2 prior lines of therapy directed against B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD19 surface antigen expression demonstrated by local laboratory assay (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active central nervous system involvement by B‑acute lymphoblastic leukemia (presence of blasts in cerebrospinal fluid). Isolated extramedullary disease relapse. Testicular leukemia. Clinically relevant preexisting central nervous system disorders including epilepsy, seizures, strokes, serious traumatic brain injury, demential illnesses, Parkinson’s disease, cerebellar disease, organic brain syndromes, or psychotic conditions. Prior grade 4 neurologic toxicities associated with cellular therapies. History of another malignant neoplasm (except for predefined acceptable exceptions). Persistent untreated or inadequately resolved adverse events graded ≥2 attributable to earlier therapies.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥60 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma of prostate (small cell carcinoma excluded). Cancer burden allowed: Radiographically measurable disease (at least one lesion ≥1 cm) or documented metastatic disease on CT, MRI, conventional bone scintigraphy, ^68Ga‑PSMA PET, or Axumin PET. Prior treatment required: NA. Required forthcoming therapy: Initiation of androgen deprivation therapy combined with docetaxel (six cycles or fewer) plus either abiraterone acetate with prednisone or darolutamide (triple therapy). Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of combination hormonal therapy that includes the same androgen receptor–targeted agent planned for the study (abiraterone acetate, enzalutamide, apalutamide, or darolutamide). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants receiving docetaxel who have metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer with an estimated time to subsequent therapy of less than 12 months.; Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).; Participation in another investigational study concurrently.; Presence of brain metastases.; Allergic reaction to substances chemically or biologically similar to study agents.; Histologic diagnosis of small cell prostate cancer.; Symptomatic skeletal event (spinal cord compression, pathologic fracture, or need for bone irradiation/surgery) within the preceding 6 months.; Uncontrolled pain attributable to prostate cancer or other chronic condition.; Visceral crisis indicating rapid disease progression with life expectancy shorter than 18 months.; Any uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness.; Active second malignant neoplasm.; Severe primary sleep disorders (e.g., narcolepsy).; Ocular conditions preventing adequate light perception (untreated cataract, severe glaucoma, macular degeneration, blindness, pupillary abnormalities, or other retinopathies).; Significant psychiatric disturbance (bipolar disorder, mania, or other severe psychosis).; Employment involving regular night‑shift work.; Prior use of light therapy for alleviating fatigue or depressive symptoms.; Recovery from intraocular surgery within the last 6 months causing eye irritation.; Photosensitivity, epilepsy, or personal history of seizures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell malignancy (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed and/or refractory disease with documented progressive disease following most recent anticancer therapy. Prior treatment required: At least one proteasome inhibitor **and** at least one immunomodulatory agent. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants must not have any known active or past central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.
Participants must not have active or historical plasma cell leukemia, Waldenström's macroglobulinemia, POEMS syndrome, or clinically significant amyloidosis.
Participants must not have a history or current presence of clinically significant central nervous system pathology such as seizures, aphasia, stroke, serious traumatic brain injury, dementia, Parkinson's disease, cerebellar disease, nor currently active psychotic illness.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Triple‑negative breast carcinoma (Estrogen receptor ≤5% and Progesterone receptor ≤5% and HER2‑negative). Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable locally advanced disease (any anatomical sites, including bone‑only involvement; treated, asymptomatic brain metastases permissible). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any chemotherapy administered for metastatic breast cancer; Irinotecan or any topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate at any time; Anti‑PD‑1, PD‑L1, or PD‑L2 agents at any time; Sacituzuman govitecan at any time; Biological therapy targeting breast cancer in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: PD‑L1‑negative (tumor‑infiltrating immune cells <1% by SP142 assay **or** Combined Positive Score <10 by 22C3 assay) **and** Estrogen receptor ≤5% **and** Progesterone receptor ≤5% **and** HER2‑negative (ASCO/CAP criteria). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis; uncontrolled cardiac failure or severe cardiovascular disease; inadequate hepatic function (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, transaminases >2.5× ULN [>5× ULN if liver metastases]); impaired renal function (creatinine >1.5× ULN or CrCl <30 ml/min); unresolved Grade ≥2 toxicity from prior therapy; active central nervous system metastases causing neurologic deficits or requiring steroids; concurrent systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalents) or other immunosuppressants; active autoimmune disease needing systemic therapy; known HIV infection; active hepatitis B or C infection; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 28 days; pregnant or lactating individuals; inability to comply with study procedures; concomitant malignant disease requiring active treatment or relapse within 3 years (except certain adequately treated in‑situ carcinomas or basal/non‑melanoma skin cancers).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both male and female. Cancer type allowed: melanoma. Histology allowed: melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stage IV metastatic melanoma or unresectable Stage III melanoma with measurable disease per RECIST 1.1 and without untreated, active, or symptomatic central nervous system metastases. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior systemic anticancer therapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma or prior anti‑PD-(L)1 or anti‑LAG‑3 therapy that resulted in a life‑threatening adverse event. Biomarkers required: HLA‑A*02:01 positivity and documented BRAF V600 mutation status (testing performed). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of a malignant disease other than melanoma; untreated, active, or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; known hypersensitivity to investigational product components; clinically significant pulmonary disease or impaired lung function; clinically significant cardiovascular disease or impaired cardiac function; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy; any uncontrolled concomitant medical condition judged to jeopardize safe participation or data integrity; inadequate contraceptive measures for persons of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Small cell carcinoma (high‑grade neuroendocrine). Cancer burden allowed: Extensive‑stage disease (distal/metastatic spread) AND no active brain or leptomeningeal metastases. Prior treatment required: Exactly one prior platinum‑containing systemic therapy line composed of ≥2 cycles, finished ≥30 days before enrollment, WITH documented radiologic disease progression after that therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior B7‑H3‑directed agent (e.g., orlatamab, enoblituzumab, or similar), ANY prior antibody‑drug conjugate bearing an exatecan derivative (e.g., trastuzumab deruxtecan), ANY prior topoisomerase I inhibitor or any therapy identified as a “treatment of physician’s choice” comparator in this study. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of (non‑infectious) interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis that required corticosteroid therapy, current or unresolved interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, clinically severe pulmonary compromise (including dependence on supplemental oxygen, prior pneumonectomy, or serious underlying pulmonary/autoinflammatory disease), uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease, cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or other major arterial thromboembolic event occurring within the preceding six months, clinically significant corneal disease, insufficient washout period before randomization as stipulated in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 12 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: NUT carcinoma. Histology allowed: NUT carcinoma identified by ectopic NUT protein expression by immunohistochemistry **or** NUT gene translocation demonstrated by fluorescent in‐situ hybridisation, DNA next‑generation sequencing, or RNA sequencing. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable disease with measurable or evaluable lesions per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: None specific; any number of prior lines of therapy in the metastatic setting—including prior BET inhibitor therapy and prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy—is permissible. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Positive NUT protein staining by IHC **or** documented NUT gene fusion/translocation by validated assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Receiving any other investigational agents; history of hypersensitivity to BET inhibitor or CDK4/6 inhibitor constituents; current use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers; inability to discontinue such agents appropriately; untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as active systemic infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmias, interstitial lung disease, resting severe dyspnea or dependence on supplemental oxygen, severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min), baseline Grade ≥ 2 diarrhoea from underlying inflammatory bowel disease; concurrent use of proton pump inhibitors; pregnancy or lactation; QTc interval ≥450 ms on screen ECG; use of Factor Xa or thrombin (Factor IIa) inhibitors (except low‑molecular‑weight heparin); prior irradiation encompassing more than 25 % of bone marrow; receipt of bone‑targeted radionuclides within six weeks before study start; myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months preceding enrolment; gastrointestinal disorders markedly impairing drug absorption; personal history of syncope of cardiac origin, sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmia, ventricular fibrillation, or sudden cardiac death.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria: Visceral crisis or impending visceral crisis.; Need for emergent neurosurgical intervention to address CNS lesions.; Immediate indication for local CNS therapy as defined by local standards.; Symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage.; More than two seizures within four weeks preceding enrolment.; Persistent grade >1 toxicities attributable to prior anticancer therapy not resolved to ≤grade 1.; Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging (unless substitute computed tomography meets measurement criteria for applicable cohorts.).; Concomitant receipt of other antitumour agents during the study.; Active uncontrolled intercurrent illness—including serious infection, markedly impaired pulmonary function, unstable angina, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction, recent gastrointestinal ulcers, advanced hepatic or renal impairment, or severe malnutrition.; Use of systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 4 mg/day prednisone equivalent (or equivalent immunosuppressants) within seven days prior to baseline imaging.; Current, historical, or suspect interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis.; Uncontrolled epilepsy, other disabling neurologic or psychiatric disorder interfering with adherence.; Any additional malignant neoplasm besides fully excised non‑melanoma skin cancer, cured in‑situ disease, or other solid tumour rendered disease‑free for ≥3 years.; Major surgery, extensive biopsies, or substantial trauma within 28 days before starting study medication.; Significant ocular surface/corneal pathology.; Severe hypersensitivity reaction to datipotamab deruxtecan components or to other monoclonal antibodies.; Pregnancy or nursing mother.; Inadequate contraception measures for participants capable of conceiving or causing conception.; Donation or banking of gametes during study and up to stipulated follow‑up period.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: any NSCLC histology. Cancer burden allowed: advanced, unresectable and/or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: progressive disease after prior platinum‑containing chemotherapy **and** checkpoint inhibitor therapy; if epidermal growth factor receptor, anaplastic lymphoma kinase, ROS1 or other actionable driver mutation present, progressive disease after corresponding FDA‑approved targeted therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS mutation (any variant) confirmed in tumor tissue or plasma **and** not having received a KRAS G12C inhibitor if the KRAS allele is G12C. Biomarkers excluded: Previous KRAS G12C inhibitor treatment in patients harboring a KRAS G12C mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active or historical pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis requiring systemic steroids; Immunodeficiency or active autoimmune disease needing chronic systemic corticosteroids >10 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent or other immunosuppression within the past 2 years; Grade ≥2 sensory peripheral neuropathy (part 2 only); Clinically significant ongoing gastrointestinal disease (incl. inflammatory bowel disease, immune‑mediated colitis, peptic ulcer disease, recent major GI bleed, chronic diarrhoea); Recent serious hemorrhage (hematemesis, massive hemoptysis, melena) within 6 months; Major surgery or radiation therapy completed ≤4 weeks ago or radiation involving >30 % bone marrow; Known hypersensitivity to study drugs or excipients; Significant hematologic, renal, or hepatic laboratory abnormalities.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Primary central nervous system lymphoma. Histology allowed: Any histology confirming primary central nervous system lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with either a measurable intracranial/parenchymal lesion (longest diameter ≥10 mm and ≤40 mm on MRI or CT within 28 days pre‑cycle 1) **or** documented leptomeningeal involvement evidenced by abnormal cerebrospinal fluid cytology/flow cytometry ± spinal imaging within 28 days pre‑cycle 1. Prior treatment required: Demonstrable disease progression after receiving a Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor (BTKi). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants with isolated intra‑ocular primary CNS lymphoma without brain or CSF involvement; T‑cell lymphomas; systemic lymphoma or non‑CNS lymphoma metastatic to the CNS; evidence of systemic lymphoma on PET/CT of chest/abdomen/pelvis (or testicular ultrasound when appropriate); active malignant disease other than PCNSL requiring systemic therapy; history of Grade ≥3 rhabdomyolysis without full recovery; need for emergent therapy owing to uncontrollable tumour bulk or edema; prior history of malignancies other than lymphoma (excluding basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas or cervical/breast carcinoma in situ) unless disease‐free ≥3 years; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation within 60 days (unless stable/tapering immunosuppression approved); known hypersensitivity to emavusertib, ibrutinib, or their excipients; inadequate life expectancy (<3 months); Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status > 2; uncontrolled concurrent infections (HIV, hepatitis B/C); significant cardiac, hepatic, or renal insufficiency; unresolved Grade ≥2 pulmonary toxicity or past immune‑mediated pneumonitis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 Years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma. Histology allowed: Squamous cell carcinoma. Prior treatment required: One to two prior lines of systemic therapy for recurrent/metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, including at least one line that contained an anti‑Programmed Death‑1 or Anti‑Programmed Death Ligand‑1 agent. general_exclusion_criteria: History of active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated, stable brain metastases permitted if off steroids ≥3 weeks). Solid organ transplantation recipient. Significant autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive therapy (e.g., prednisone ≥10 mg/day). Recent serious cardiovascular events (symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, stroke, or life‑threatening arrhythmias within past six months). Ongoing uncontrolled infection. Presence of another actively progressing malignant neoplasm (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, in‑situ cervical cancer). Positive serologic tests for Hepatitis B surface antigen or Hepatitis C antibodies (unless HCV RNA negative). Pregnant or nursing individuals, or persons unwilling to practice effective contraception throughout study participation and for stipulated post‑treatment interval.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: any cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: immune checkpoint inhibitor‐induced colitis grade 2‑3. Prior treatment required: receipt of an immune checkpoint inhibitor for cancer within the past 8 weeks. Prior treatment excluded: prior history of inflammatory colitis requiring >10 mg prednisone daily or any immunosuppressive medication. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Colonic perforation or abscess.
Partial or complete bowel obstruction within the last 3 months, associated signs/symptoms, or radiographic evidence of impending obstruction.
Active Clostridioides difficile or other colonic infection.
Concurrent hepatitis B or C infection.
History of untreated tuberculosis and/or positive quantitative interferon gamma release assay without prior tuberculostatic prophylaxis, or untreated active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Active or prior infection with nontuberculous mycobacteria.
Unable or unwilling to undergo colonoscopy or flexible sigmoidoscopy.
Requirement to remain inpatient for planned treatment (participants must be outpatient when receiving study therapy).
History of total proctocolectomy.
Female participants who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or planning pregnancy within the next 6 months.
Incapacity to provide informed consent.
Recent SARS‑CoV‑2 infection (<=10 days for mild disease or <=20 days for severe/critical illness) preceding first Vedolizumab administration.
Allergy to sulfamethoxazole‑trimethoprim.
Body weight exceeding 120 kilograms.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Intake of herbal or dietary supplements known to pose substantial hepatotoxic risk.
Administration of drugs associated with marked QTc prolongation or torsades de pointes risk within fourteen days of initiating study treatment.
Presence of unresolved grade ≥ 2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia).
Active uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including acute liver injury, poorly controlled chronic liver disease, or established cirrhosis.
Active or insufficiently managed alcohol‑use disorder.
Pregnancy or current breastfeeding.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of a locoregional tumor amenable to potentially curative surgical resection, definitive radiotherapy, or chemotherapy.  
* Any concurrent second active malignancy (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ).  
* Evidence of severe acute toxicities persisting beyond Grade 1 according to CTCAE despite recovery attempts.  
* Significant cardiovascular adverse event occurring within six months preceding enrollment.  
* Documented untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.  
* Current interstitial lung disease necessitating therapeutic intervention.  
* Past or current inflammatory eye diseases such as uveitis, retinitis, or serious corneal pathology.  
* Recent major surgery resulting in unhealed wounds (within 30 days) or minor procedures (within 10 days).  
* Positive testing for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, active Hepatitis B, or active Hepatitis C infection.  
* Previous participation in a clinical investigation involving a selective or nonspecific CDK2 inhibitor or degraders.  
* Liver impairment classified as Child‑Pugh B or C, or any other substantial hepatic insufficiency.  
* Receipt of palliative external beam radiation within fourteen days or any form of radiation therapy within four weeks before day 1 of study medication administration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 1 year and younger than 25 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (precursor B‑cell). Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease with ≥25 % blasts in bone marrow aspiration (or equivalent diagnostic evidence); for patients aged 1‑9.99 years, white blood cell count must be either ≥50,000/µL **or** <50,000/µL accompanied by testicular leukemia **or** central nervous system leukemia (CNS3) **or** steroid pretreatment; for patients aged 10‑24.99 years any white blood cell count is permissible. Prior treatment required: none (no prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for the current diagnosis except allowable steroid pretreatment or intrathecal cytarabine). Prior treatment excluded: any prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for the current diagnosis; hydroxyurea exposure >72 hours within the preceding 7 days. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: MYC translocation associated with mature (Burkitt) B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with Down syndrome; patients lacking adequate diagnostic bone marrow or peripheral blood samples meeting minimal cellularity thresholds; patients with acute undifferentiated leukemia; for B‑lymphoblastic lymphoma cases, exclusion of T‑lymphoblastic lymphoma, morphologically unclassifiable lymphoma, or specimens lacking both B‑cell and T‑cell marker expression; individuals with known Charcot‑Marie‑tooth disease; necessity for radiotherapy at diagnosis; female participants who are pregnant; lactating women planning breastfeeding through study drug exposure and for two months thereafter; sexually active persons of reproductive potential unwilling to employ effective contraception throughout study participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: any. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia (lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma). Histology allowed: lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: disease fulfilling International Workshop on Waldenström’s Macroglobulinemia version 2 treatment‐initiation criteria (symptomatic disease such as hyperviscosity, peripheral neuropathy, cold agglutinin disease, cryoglobulinemia, amyloid deposition, cytopenias from marrow infiltration, bulky nodal masses, symptomatic splenomegaly, unexplained constitutional symptoms, or end‑organ impairment) and serum immunoglobulin M > 2 times the upper limit of normal. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of therapy that included BOTH an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody (e.g., rituximab) AND a brutonʼs tyrosine kinase inhibitor (e.g., ibrutinib, zanubrutinib), irrespective of order; receipt of both drugs concurrently as frontline therapy satisfies this requirement. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: tumour CD20 positivity by immunophenotype or immunohistochemistry and serum IgM >2 × ULN. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Transformed aggressive lymphoma.
• Symptomatic or refractory hyperviscosity syndrome with IgM >4000 mg/dL when plasma exchange cannot be performed promptly.
• Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study.
• Residual toxicities >Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
• Active uncontrolled infection necessitating inpatient care or IV antibiotics within 4 weeks.
• Significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA Class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction <45 %, unstable angina, myocardial infarction <6 months, serious arrhythmias).
• Serious central nervous system disorders (uncontrolled seizures, recent stroke, psychosis, dementia, CNS vasculitis, encephalitis).
• Requirement for supplemental oxygen at rest to maintain SpO₂ > 92 %.
• Recent (>28 days) systemic glucocorticoids ≥20 mg prednisolone equivalent daily or other chronic immunosuppressants for non‑study reasons.
• Documented central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia (CNS lesions, leptomeningeal disease) or untreated CNS disease from another malignancy.
• Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to comply with mandated highly effective contraception throughout study and for stipulated post‑treatment periods.
• Current alcohol/substance misuse, significant psychiatric illness, or unstable psychosocial circumstances limiting protocol adherence.
• Known hypersensitivity to epcoritamab or structurally related antibodies.
• Receipt of a live or live‑attenuated vaccination within 4 weeks preceding enrollment.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiac or vascular disease within three months. Recent serious infections requiring hospitalization or IV antibiotics within 28 days before dosing. Active uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection. Presence of any myeloma‑defining events. Diagnosed systemic AL amyloidosis, Waldenström macroglobulinaemia (lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma), solitary plasmacytoma, or symptomatic multiple myeloma. Poor ECOG performance status (> 1). Significant hepatic impairment or inadequate hematologic parameters beyond protocol specifications. Severe renal insufficiency (estimated GFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m²).

Cancer burden allowed: measurable disease defined as ≥1 nodal lesion long axis >1.5 cm or short axis >1.0 cm, or ≥1 extranodal lesion long axis >1.0 cm on PET/CT/MRI, FDG‐avid. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anti‑lymphoma therapy (e.g., chemotherapy, immunochemotherapy, Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor, CAR‑T cellular therapy, autologous/allogeneic stem‑cell transplantation); prior localized radiotherapy or short‑course corticosteroids are permitted. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known central nervous system involvement; active infection requiring systemic antimicrobials; major surgery or significant trauma within 4 weeks before starting study drug; requirement for warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist; history of severe inherited bleeding disorders or spontaneous bleeding necessitating transfusion; ischemic stroke or intracranular hemorrhage within 6 months; life‑threatening hemorrhage within 3 months; uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia unless attributable to lymphoma; active hepatitis C infection (except untreated cases meeting strict virologic thresholds); active hepatitis B infection (with detectable HBV DNA); prior malignant disease unless cured and disease‑free ≥3 years (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, Gleason 6 prostate cancer under surveillance); NYHA class III/IV congestive heart failure; myocardial infarction within 6 months; unstable angina within 3 months; uncontrolled arrhythmia or ventricular tachyarrhythmias within 6 months; higher‑grade atrioventricular block without pacing; uncontrollable hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg and diastolic > 105 mmHg on two readings); baseline corrected QT interval > 480 ms; inability to swallow capsules or significant GI malabsorption; concomitant use of strong CYP3A inducer medicines; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study; any severe/uncontrolled comorbidity jeopardizing safe participation; inability to comply with protocol mandates.
