Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: female and male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: infiltrating breast carcinoma that is estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: locoregional advancement deemed locally advanced or distant metastatic disease (any sites, including asymptomatic treated brain metastasis). Prior treatment required: documented disease progression on an aromatase inhibitor combined with palbociclib **or** ribociclib administered as first-line endocrine therapy for metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: disease progression on abemaciclib **or** fulvestrant **or** any selective estrogen receptor degrader; prior visceral crisis necessitating urgent cytotoxic chemotherapy. Biomarkers required: detection of at least one point mutation in the estrogen receptor 1 (ESR1) ligand‑binding domain identified in circulating tumor DNA or tumor tissue; estrogen receptor positive and HER2 negative status confirmed. Biomarkers excluded: RB1 gene mutation or deletion.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of lymphangitic carcinomatosis involving the lungs; prior grade 3 or grade 4 interstitial lung disease; known hypersensitivity to fulvestrant or formulation components; recent therapeutic radiotherapy (within 30 days) without adequate recovery; congenital or acquired long QTc syndrome or baseline QTc > 480 ms; unresolved thromboembolic events (pulmonary embolism, deep venous thrombosis) unless occurring >6 months ago and managed with chronic anticoagulation; cardiovascular conditions increasing venous thromboembolism risk such as severe congestive heart failure; concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducers; clinically significant comorbidities affecting absorption or overall health; active serious bacterial or fungal infection requiring intravenous antimicrobial therapy; known infection with HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C; additional malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the preceding five years (except adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer); positive urine pregnancy test in premenopausal participants; lack of reliable dual barrier contraception usage when sexually active; lactation/breastfeeding; demonstrated non‑adherence to medication regimes; inability or unwillingness to comply with study protocols; enrollment in another investigational drug or device study within the last thirty days.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ANY AGE. Sex allowed: BOTH. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma (CD20‑positive large B‑cell lymphoma). Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma, not otherwise classified. Cancer burden allowed: International Prognostic Index score 2‑5 AND ECOG Performance Status 0‑2 AND at least one bidimensional measurable lesion ≥1.5 cm. Prior treatment required: NONE (untreated). Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic chemotherapy, prior immunochemotherapy, prior monoclonal antibody therapy for cancer, prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell or bispecific antibody therapy, prior radiotherapy to mediastinal or pericardial regions, prior autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplantation, prior systemic immunosuppressive agents administered within 4 weeks, prior use of any monoclonal antibody for cancer within 3 months, prior participation in an investigative anticancer trial within 28 days, prior corticosteroid administration exceeding 30 mg prednisone equivalents per day for indications other than lymphoma symptom control. Biomarkers required: Tumour demonstrated CD20 positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Contraindication or hypersensitivity to any component of Pola‑R‑CHP or glofitamab; prior solid organ transplantation; current peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1; significant or extensive cardiovascular disease; recent major surgery within 4 weeks; active or unresolved central nervous system disease unrelated to lymphoma (e.g., stroke, seizure disorder, vascular inflammation, degenerative neurologic condition); chronic active Epstein‑Barr virus infection; haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy; clinically significant liver disease; live attenuated vaccinations within 4 weeks preceding enrollment or anticipated thereafter until B‑cell recovery; any active infection within 7 days impacting safety; suspicion or evidence of active or latent tuberculosis; positive serology for chronic hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or Human T‑lymphotropic Virus‑1; history of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.

Active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C requiring ongoing antiviral therapy.

Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Definite clinical or radiographic evidence of metastatic disease  
• Tumors staged pT4, including inflammatory breast cancer  
• History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer (previous DCIS or LCIS alone is allowed)  
• Life expectancy less than ten years owing to comorbid conditions  
• Laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count <1200/mm³, platelet count <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <10 g/dL, total bilirubin >Upper Limit of Normal (or >1.5×ULN if related to benign hyperbilirubinemia), ALT or AST >3 times Upper Limit of Normal, glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min/1.73 m²  
• Cardiac disease placing the patient above New York Heart Association functional classification 2B  
• Non‑epithelial breast malignancies such as sarcoma or lymphoma  
• Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraception compliance  
• Active chronic hepatitis B infection with detectable viral load despite therapy, or unresolved hepatitis C infection (unless successfully treated/cured or suppressed)  
• Uncontrolled intercurrent illness judged by the investigator to impede protocol adherence or data interpretation.

Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic > 180 mmHg or diastolic > 110 mmHg). Severe concurrent pulmonary pathology such as pulmonary embolism within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusion, interstitial lung disease, or autoimmune/inflammatory disorders involving the lungs; prior total pneumonectomy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Participation in another therapeutic clinical trial (aside from translational studies).
• Active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (patients with historically treated, stable CNS lesions ok).
• Presence of a concurrent malignant disease or a malignancy diagnosed within the preceding five years, excluding cervical carcinoma in situ, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or Stage I uterine cancer.
• Known allergic or hypersensitivity reactions to investigational agents or excipients.
• Recent palliative radiotherapy to a limited field (<2 weeks ago) or extensive-field/wide-field (>30 % bone marrow) radiotherapy within four weeks.
• Major surgery or significant trauma within fourteen days prior to starting study treatment, or anticipated need for major surgery during the study.
• Significant cardiovascular conditions: unstable angina, myocardial infarction, NYHA class II–IV congestive heart failure within six months, poorly controlled hypertension (SBP ≥ 180 mmHg or DBP ≥ 100 mmHg), left ventricular ejection fraction < 55 %, symptomatic arrhythmias requiring intervention, prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms females, >450 ms males), or use of prohibited QT‑prolonging medicines.
• Severe pulmonary comorbidity—including recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusions, post‑COVID‑19 fibrotic changes—or prior pneumonectomy.
• Past or current interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis needing steroid therapy, or unresolved suspicion of ILD/pneumonitis at screening.
• Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to employ adequate contraception according to protocol specifications.
• Active hepatitis B or C infection (resolved/resolved carriers acceptable per detailed virologic criteria).
• Human immunodeficiency virus infection or primary immunodeficiency.
• Ongoing uncontrolled infectious processes.
• Administration of live or attenuated vaccinations within thirty days before first dose.
• History of uncontrolled seizures, destabilizing neurologic disorders, or serious unmanaged psychiatric illness impairing adherence.
• Any additional severe or uncontrolled medical condition deemed unsuitable by the investigators, including substance misuse.

• Inability or unwillingness to comply fully with protocol requirements.

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain; history of greater than Grade 2 intracranial haemorrhage; concomitant use of warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist derivatives within seven days of study start; arterial thromboembolic event such as stroke within three months preceding enrolment; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive medication (prednisone > 10 mg/day or equivalent) or systemic treatment within the past two years; necessity for high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dex­ametasones > 4 mg/day or equivalent for more than three consecutive days) within two weeks of registration; receipt of systemic anti‑cancer therapy—including investigational agents—too recently according to protocol specifications; major surgical procedure within two weeks before initiating study treatment; incomplete recovery from prior radiation‑related toxicities, ongoing requirement for corticosteroids, or recent radiation pneumonitis; recent cranial irradiation within two weeks of study commencement; administration of a live vaccine within thirty days before first dose; existence of another actively progressing malignancy or one requiring treatment within the past three years (with exceptions for cured cutaneous carcinomas and similar lesions); severe hypersensitivity reaction (grade ≥ 3) to study drugs or excipients; history of non‑infectious pneumonitis necessitating steroids or existing pneumonitis; uncontrolled acute systemic infection graded higher than CTCAE 2 or completion of intravenous antimicrobial therapy less than fourteen days ago; clinically significant cardiovascular disease such as uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure NYHA class III–IV, unstable angina, or myocardial infarction within six months; uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized therapy; inadequately managed diabetes mellitus with glycated hemoglobin > 8%; non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or bone fractures; inherent bleeding diatheses (e.g., von Willebrand disease, hemophilia); conditions preventing swallowing of capsules or causing marked gastrointestinal malabsorption; concurrent intake of potent/moderate CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers not appropriately withheld; usage of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants without appropriate substitution; known human immunodeficiency virus/acquired immunodeficiency syndrome; active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA); active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection; any comorbidity, therapy, or laboratory abnormality that could compromise safety or data integrity; underlying immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding ten milligrams prednisone equivalents per day; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; untreated serious psychiatric illness or substance misuse jeopardising adherence; pregnancy, lactation, intention to become pregnant, or plans to sire a child throughout the study period and until twelve hundred eighty days (approximately 120 days) after the final dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for invasive breast cancer (chemotherapy, targeted therapy, hormonally directed therapy, or immunotherapy) undertaken before study entry, except when consisting exclusively of trastuzumab + pertuzumab + taxane administered ≤6 weeks prior to enrollment; prior investigational agents for this condition. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression defined as Immunohistochemistry 3+ on both primary tumour and metastatic specimen according to ASCO/CAP guideline. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of myocardial infarction within 6 months before enrollment  
Symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class II–IV)  
Significant arrhythmias requiring treatment (grade ≥ 3) or uncontrolled atrial fibrillation  
Untreated central nervous system lesions on baseline MRI (unless cleared by sponsor investigator)  
Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid use exceeding 2 mg dexamethasone equivalents daily for brain metastasis symptom control  
Leptomeningeal disease evident on imaging  
Frequent uncontrolled seizures (> once per week)  
Past or current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis necessitating steroids or unable to rule out by imaging  
Other substantial pulmonary comorbidities (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusions)  
Active autoimmune, connective‑tissue, or inflammatory disorders with pulmonary involvement  
Previous pneumonectomy  
Unresolved acute or serious infection  
Inability to ingest oral capsules/tablets or gastrointestinal pathology impairing drug absorption  
Grade ≥ 2 diarrhea of any cause at screening  
Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 2C8 or 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers  
Pregnancy or lactation  

(Note: All timing‑specific washout periods have been omitted as instructed.)

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional investigative study.
Clinically significant third‑space fluid collections (ascites or pleural effusion) unless confirmed malignant by cytology.
Known central nervous system lymphoma.
Active human immunodeficiency virus infection, chronic hepatitis B or C requiring treatment.
Unstable angina within six months, myocardial infarction within six months, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, or uncontrolled/symptomatic arrhythmias.
History of Stevens‑Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis.
Use of systemic immunosuppressive medications concurrently.
Administration of live, attenuated vaccines within four weeks of study entry.
Recent serious infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy (completed less than fourteen days before dosing) – omitted when solely a timed washout.
Ongoing substance/alcohol dependence interfering with compliance.
Past non‑lymphoma malignancy, except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, localized prostate cancer, or other Stage I‑II solid tumors now in complete remission.
Persistent grade higher than 1 adverse events attributable to prior therapy (excluding any-grade alopecia and up to grade 2 peripheral neuropathy).
Significant preexisting hepatic impairment, including cirrhosis or viral/autoinflammatory hepatitis.
Inadequate ability or willingness to comply with study procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe brain injury or stroke.; Unable to undergo scheduled serial magnetic resonance imaging assessments.; History of epileptic disorders or seizures unrelated to the tumor.; Concomitant use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers.; Current or prior interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced interstitial lung disease, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids.; Significant untreated/comorbid respiratory diseases such as severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.; Previous exposure to known pneumotoxic chemotherapeutic agents resulting in persistent lung toxicity.; Documented myopathy or persistently elevated serum creatine kinase >5× ULN.; Recent major cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction ≤6 months), New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or serious ventricular arrhythmia.; Active severe pulmonary infection or uncontrolled infectious disease (including active tuberculosis, chronic hepatitis B/C when uncontrolled).; Unresolved adverse event grades higher than NCI CTCAE version 4.03 grade 1 at baseline (chronic grade 2 toxicities permitted only if medically manageable).

Prior treatment required: Prior exposure to a proteasome inhibitor **and** an immunomodulatory drug **and** an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody; having received 1 to 4 prior lines of systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major concurrent illness or organ dysfunction—including plasma cell leukemia (≥5% circulating plasma cells), Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia, POEMS syndrome, and primary amyloid light‑chain amyloidosis. History of allergy or known hypersensitivity to any trial therapy or its excipients, or contraindications per labeling. Complete spinal cord compression or central nervous system involvement. Active or historical autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immune suppression within two years (exceptions apply for Type 1 diabetes, vitiligo, psoriasis, thyroid disorders not requiring immunosuppression). Receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding dosing. Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease after allogeneic transplantation requiring >20 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent steroids. Active infections requiring treatment. Any medical condition, therapy, or lab abnormality judged to potentially confound trial outcomes or impede completion. Significant psychiatric or substance‑use disorders impairing compliance. Legal incapacity or lack of sufficient legal authority. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Estrogen receptor positive, HER2 negative breast carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy in the advanced/metastatic setting; prior elacestrant; prior alpelisib. general_exclusion_criteria: Active or untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; Symptomatic visceral crises such as massive uncontrollable effusions, pulmonary lymphangitic spread, or >50% hepatic involvement; Prior chemotherapy or prior elacestrant in the metastatic setting; Germline BRCA mutation without prior receipt of a PARP inhibitor; Concurrent malignant neoplasm or invasive cancer diagnosis within three years (excluding adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia); Ongoing clinically significant infections; Hepatitis B/C viremic infection; Untreated HIV infection; Prior pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; Major surgical procedure within 28 days before initiation; Conditions impairing oral intake or drug absorption (severe gastrectomy, malabsorptive syndromes, persistent vomiting); Known hypersensitivity to elacestrant or formulation components; Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or inadequate contraception in persons of reproductive potential; Failure to comply with donor restriction policies for gametes; Recent use of prohibited concomitant medicines (strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducers or inhibitors, specified herbals, live vaccines, anti‑cancer agents within stipulated intervals); Current alcohol or illicit drug dependence; Significant cardiovascular, respiratory, hematologic, hepatic, or renal disorders deemed unsafe by investigators.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: High‑grade serous ovarian cancer, High‑grade serous fallopian tube cancer, High‑grade serous primary peritoneal cancer. Histology allowed: High‑grade serous adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Platinum‑resistant disease with measurable lesions per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: Prior bevacizumab and prior mirvetuximab if eligible per standard of care and 1–3 prior systemic therapy lines (allows up to 4 lines if prior mirvetuximab); Prior PARP inhibitor (required because BRCA1/2 mutation or homologous recombination deficiency is present). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Cyclin E1 positive by immunohistochemistry and (BRCA1/2 mutation **or** homologous recombination deficiency). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Primary platinum‑refractory disease. Recent major surgery (within 28 days). Recent hospitalization (within 14 days). Chemotherapy or targeted anticancer therapy administered within 21 days (or five half‑lives). Radiotherapy administered within 21 days. Autologous or allogenic stem cell transplantation within 3 months. Concurrent use of any other investigational drug within 28 days (or five half‑lives). Inability to stop strong/moderate CYP3A inhibitors/inducers or P‑glycoprotein modulators at least 14 days before first dose. Previous treatment with ZN‑c3 or any WEE1, ATR, PKMYT1, or CHK1/2 inhibitor. Untreated or symptomatic brain metastases/leptomeningeal disease (stable/asymptomatic brain metastases allowable). Histology allowed: any non‑small cell lung cancer histology excluding mixed small‑cell lung cancer and large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease (locally advanced or metastatic) with measurable lesion(s) per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one and no more than three prior lines of systemic anti‑cancer therapy administered in the advanced/metastatic setting with documented disease progression. Prior treatment excluded: prior receipt of any KRAS G12C inhibitor or any pan‑KRAS/RAS inhibitor. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C mutation positive (documented on available tumour tissue obtained for screening). Biomarkers excluded: presence of any other concurrently targetable oncogenic driver alteration.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known hypersensitivity to components of investigational drugs. Malabsorption syndromes or conditions impairing gastrointestinal absorption. Active or untreated central nervous system metastases. Leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis. Uncontrolled pleural, pericardial, or abdominal effusions requiring repeated drainage twice weekly or more. Ongoing infection judged to affect safety, including receiving therapeutic oral or intravenous antibiotics shortly before enrollment. Exposure to greater than 30 Gray thoracic radiotherapy within six months preceding randomisation. Uncontrolled tumor‑related pain. Residual grade ≥2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapies. Recent major illness affecting organ function (cardiac, hepatic, renal, pulmonary). History of another malignant neoplasm within five years prior to screening, except for adequately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, non‑melanoma skin cancer, localized prostate cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, or stage I uterine cancer.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
Additional primary malignancy within three years prior to enrollment (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, low‑grade prostate cancer)
Active primary central nervous system malignancy
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within twelve months preceding enrollment
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease within six months or historic congestive heart failure
Severe acute infections including ongoing viral hepatitis B/C, HIV infection, or untreated tuberculosis
Live attenuated vaccination within four weeks prior to enrollment or anticipated thereafter until after lymphodepletion
Primary immunodeficiency disorders
Ongoing autoimmune disease requiring systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressants (aside from residual effects of prior checkpoint inhibition)
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or women of childbearing potential without adequate contraception
Significant concurrent medical conditions compromising safety or compliance as judged by investigator.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant cardiovascular disease (unstable cardiac function).
Uncontrolled pulmonary conditions.
Active infections including untreated HIV, hepatitis B/C, active CMV infection.
Recent (<6 weeks) administration of live vaccines.
Chronic liver disease, veno‑occlusive disease, ongoing alcohol misuse.
Renal impairment beyond protocol limits (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
Severe concurrent illnesses or organ dysfunction precluding safe participation.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent use of prohibited concomitant medications (strong CYP3A modulators) without proper management.

Requirement for continuous prophylactic antimicrobials unavailable or inability to comply.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Estrogen receptor‑positive **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative (ER⁺/HER2‑neg) invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II‑III disease with no clinically apparent local or distant recurrence (**AJCC 7ᵗʰ edition**) **and** presence of disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow identified by central immunohistochemical assay. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive surgery **plus** any prescribed chemotherapy **and** radiation; currently receiving adjuvant endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor acceptable; tamoxifen must be stopped ≥21 days before hydroxychloroquine allocation); optional prior exposure to a CDK4/6 inhibitor **other than** palbociclib (discontinued ≥6 months before screening); continuous use of approved bone‑modifying agents (bisphosphonates or denosumab) allowable. Prior treatment excluded: Participation in another interventional oncology trial **or** concurrent administration of any investigational anticancer therapy; tamoxifen coadministered with hydroxychloroquine (requires switch to aromatase inhibitor). Biomarkers required: Detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by immunohistochemistry (screened centrally) **and** confirmation of ER⁺/**and** HER2‑neg status on the primary tumor specimen. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of prior invasive breast cancer (except DCIS >5 years earlier); prior invasive non‑breast malignancies requiring disease‑free interval <5 years; chronic high‑dose systemic steroid use or other potent immunosuppressives; QTc prolongation >480 ms; uncontrolled cardiac disease—including symptomatic arrhythmias (unless well‑controlled atrial fibrillation), acute coronary syndrome or PCI/stent placement <6 months, NYHA class II or higher heart failure; history of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease or DLCO <50 % predicated or resting oxygen sat ≤88 %; uncontrolled diabetes mellitus; active severe bacterial, viral, fungal, or opportunistic infection; moderate/severe hepatic impairment (cirrhosis, active/chronic hepatitis B/C); HIV infection receiving antiretroviral therapy; significant gastrointestinal disorder impairing oral drug absorption; active bleeding diathesis or therapeutic anticoagulation; past retinopathy or retinal vascular occlusive disease; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraceptive measures in persons of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: squamous. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic (stage IV). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: Programmed death‑ligand 1 tumour proportion score greater than 50% measured by FDA‑cleared 22C3 immunohistochemistry assay. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of any actionable genomic alteration (e.g., epidermal growth factor receptor sensitising mutation, Anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ROS1 rearrangement, BRAF V600E mutation, MET exon 14 skipping alteration, RET fusion, neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinase fusion).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of small‑cell lung carcinoma histology. Receipt of any prior therapy for non‑small cell lung cancer in the metastatic setting. Enrollment in another interventional clinical study concurrently. Symptomatic central nervous system metastases, cerebral lesions ≥1.5 cm, recent (<7 days) cranial radiotherapy, anticipated need for early intracranial irradiation, or leptomeningeal disease. Prior malignant neoplasm (other than fully resected disease without recurrence for ≥3 years). Active autoimmune disorder or active pulmonary disease requiring systemic medication. Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2. Serious infection occurring ≤4 weeks before enrolment. Recent major surgery or significant traumatic injury within ≤4 weeks preceding registration. Past episode of non‑infectious pneumonia necessitating systemic glucocorticoids or existing interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic (stage IV). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: Programmed death‑ligand 1 tumour proportion score greater than 50% measured by FDA‑cleared 22C3 immunohistochemistry assay. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of any actionable genomic alteration (e.g., epidermal growth factor receptor sensitising mutation, Anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ROS1 rearrangement, BRAFT599E mutation, MET exon 14 skipping alteration, RET fusion, neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinase fusion); Small‑cell lung carcinoma histology.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of small‑cell lung carcinoma histology. Receipt of any prior therapy for non‑small cell lung cancer in the metastatic setting. Enrollment in another interventional clinical study concurrently. Symptomatic central nervous system metastases, cerebral lesions ≥1.5 cm, recent (<7 days) cranial radiotherapy, anticipated need for early intracranial irradiation, or leptomeningeal disease. Prior malignant neoplasm (other than fully resected disease without recurrence for ≥3 years). Active autoimmune disorder or active pulmonary disease requiring systemic medication. Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2. Serious infection occurring ≤4 weeks before enrolment. Recent major surgery or significant traumatic injury within ≤4 weeks preceding registration. Past episode of non‑infectious pneumonia necessitating systemic glucocorticoids or existing interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18–65 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: first remission or beyond with ≤5 % marrow blasts, no circulating blasts, no extramedullary disease; Karnofsky performance status ≥70 %; left ventricular ejection fraction ≥45 %; estimated creatinine clearance ≥45 mL/min; DLCO >50 % and FEV1 >50 %; normal liver function tests. Prior treatment required: scheduled myeloablative conditioning regimen; receipt of a partially HLA‑matched (4/8–7/8) unrelated donor peripheral blood stem cell graft; donor age 18–40 years. Prior treatment excluded: prior allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant; autologous transplant within 3 months. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Suitable fully HLA‑matched related donor or 8/8 matched unrelated donor available.
Inability or refusal to provide informed consent or to comply with study procedures/follow‑up.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection at time of conditioning.
Concurrent participation in another prophylactic graft‑versus‑host disease or infectious disease prevention trial.
Desensitization therapy undertaken to reduce anti‑donor HLA antibodies pre‑transplant.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with persistent viremia (detectable viral load).

general_exclusion_criteria: Inability to undergo contrast‑enhanced magnetic resonance imaging.; Known allergy to tumor necrosis factor α, lomustine, any excipients, or intravenous human proteins/peptides/antibodies.; Baseline absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 × 10⁹/L, platelet count < 100 × 10⁹/L, or hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL.; Creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min or serum creatinine > 1.5 times upper limit of normal.; Liver enzymes ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase ≥ 2.5 × ULN or total bilirubin ≥ 2.0 × ULN.; International normalized ratio > 1.5 × ULN.; Significant cardiovascular disease including recent cerebrovascular event, acute coronary syndrome, myocardial infarction, unstable or severe angina, NYHA class II–III heart failure, serious arrhythmia, left ventricular ejection fraction < 55%, uncontrolled hypertension, arterial aneurysm at high risk of rupture, ischemic peripheral vascular disease grade IIb–IV.; Current or past major depression, bipolar disorder I or II, OCD, schizophrenia, suicide attempts or ideation, homicide ideation, or severe personality disorder.; Anxiety grade ≥ CTCAE 3.; Severe diabetic retinopathy (severe non‑proliferative or proliferative).; Recent major trauma or major surgery (< 4 weeks).; Positive history of tuberculosis.; Pregnant or breastfeeding.; Chronic high-dose steroids or other immunosuppressants (> 4 mg/day dexamethasone-equivalent for more than 7 days pre‑treatment).; Active, uncontrolled infection or other severe concurrent disease posing undue risk.; Concomitant malignancy unless disease free ≥ 2 years.; Administration of growth factors or immunomodulators within 7 days preceding study treatment.; Non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or fractures.; Ongoing anticoagulation with P2Y12 inhibitors or vitamin K antagonists.; Required simultaneous use of additional anticancer therapies.; Live vaccinations within 4 weeks prior to treatment or planned during study.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia, stable endocrinopathies up to Grade 2, and skin toxicity up to Grade 2).
• Concurrent receipt of any other investigational anticancer agent.
• Known hypersensitivity reaction attributable to peposertib, M1774, or substances with similar structure.
• Requirement to continue concomitant strongly inhibiting or inducing CYP3A4/5, CYP2C19, CYP2C9 agents, or narrow‑therapeutic‑index substrates of CYPs, P‑gp, BCRP, OCT1, OATP1B1/B3 when discontinuation is impossible.
• Unable to discontinue proton‑pump inhibitor usage permanently throughout protocol participation.
• Active uncontrolled medical disorders—including ongoing serious infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or persistent indwelling drainage devices.
• Baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) exceeding 470 milliseconds.
• Current pregnancy or nursing (breast feeding).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma of the central nervous system (including primary and secondary CNS lymphoma). Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma diagnosed from brain biopsy, cerebrospinal fluid cytology, or vitreous biopsy. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent or refractory disease after at least one line of CNS‑directed therapy. Prior treatment required: At least one line of CNS‑directed prior therapy and recovery to ≤grade 1 toxicity from prior therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior exposure to Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain  
* Greater than grade 2 intracranial hemorrhage  
* Active systemic disease or uncontrolled intercurrent illness  
* Prior exposure to Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitors (already noted as excluded)  
* Current second malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous cell carcinoma of skin, in‑situ cervical cancer, or other cancer disease‑free ≥3 years)  
* Significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction within 6 months, NYHA class III‑IV, or QTc > 480 msec)  
* Inability to swallow oral medications or gastrointestinal conditions limiting absorption  
* Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection, prior progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy, or any active serious infection (bacterial, viral, fungal)  
* Known hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reactions to acalabrutinib or its ingredients  
* Active bleeding diatheses (hemophilia, von Willebrand disease) or uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia / idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura  
* Necessity for concomitant strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers  
* Ongoing anticoagulation with warfarin or other vitamin K antagonists  
* Coagulation abnormalities: PT/INR or aPTT > 2 times upper limit of normal (excluding lupus anticoagulants)  
* Cerebrovascular events (stroke or intracranial hemorrhage) within 6 months preceding first dose  
* Major surgical procedure within 28 days prior to first dose (requiring full recovery)  
* Hepatitis B core antibody positive with detectable HBV DNA or hepatitis B surface antigen positivity; hepatitis C antibody positive with detectable HCV RNA  
* Pregnant or nursing women  
* Simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial  
* Advanced liver disease classified as Child‑Pugh score C  
* Uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized medical therapy

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥10 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (non‑central nervous system). Histology allowed: Any solid malignant histology. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Receipt of all available standard therapy, intolerance to available therapies, or determination by investigator that standard therapy is inappropriate. Prior treatment excluded: Prior RAF/BRAF inhibitors targeting Class 2 BRAF alterations for advanced disease; prior MEK inhibitor therapy. Biomarkers required: Presence of a BRAF gene fusion identified in tumor tissue or circulating nucleic acid by validated DNA or RNA sequencing assay. Biomarkers excluded: Co‑occurring NF1 alteration; co‑occurring RAS‐related mutations; subclonal mutations suggestive of prior treatment effect; activating RAS mutation at any time.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses limiting study participation.
Active infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection (unless special exception granted).
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or disease affecting drug absorption (e.g., ulcerative conditions, severe nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive intestinal resections).
Current active liver disease of any etiology, including seropositivity for hepatitis B surface antigen or hepatitis C antibodies confirmed by viral load testing.
Laboratory abnormalities of alanine aminotransferase, aspartate aminotransferase, γ‑glutamyltransferase or total bilirubin graded ≥ 2 that are deemed secondary to preceding immune‑checkpoint inhibitor therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: clear cell renal cell carcinoma (allowed sarcomatoid or rhabdoid differentiation). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease with at least one bone metastasis not previously irradiated; visceral metastases permitted; treated brain metastases permitted if stable. Prior treatment required: osteoclast‑directed therapy (intravenous bisphosphate or denosumab) unless medically contraindicated. Prior treatment excluded: cabozantinib; radium‑223 dichloride; hemibody external radiotherapy; systemic radiotherapies such as samarium or strontium. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure, symptomatic coronary artery disease, unstable angina, serious ventricular arrhythmias, or uncontrolled hypertension (>150/100 mmHg).
Recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident, or transient ischemic attack.
Active infectious diseases including unresolved bacterial, fungal, parasitic, or viral infections; HIV infection with detectable viral load; active hepatitis B or C not meeting suppression/cure criteria.
Significant bleeding disorder, recent major hemorrhage, or coagulopathy; current therapeutic anticoagulation with vitamin K antagonists, direct thrombin inhibitors, or disallowed antiplatelets.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh class B or C) or elevated bilirubin >1.5× upper limit of normal.
Renal insufficiency requiring dialysis or calculated creatinine clearance <30 ml/min.
Untreated central nervous system malignancy, leptomeningeal disease, or progressive/unstable brain metastases.
Imminent or existing spinal cord compression or pathological fractures pending urgent surgical stabilization.
Serious gastrointestinal pathology predisposing to perforation or fistula (active Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis flare, diverticulitis, peptic ulcer bleed, obstructive jaundice, pancreatitis).
Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial or receiving investigational anticancer therapies.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to employ adequate contraception.

Prior treatment excluded: any prior antimyeloma therapy (chemotherapy, immunomodulatory agent, proteasome inhibitor, glucocorticoid‐based regimen, or other disease‑directed therapy for multiple myeloma). Biomarkers required: serum M protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL **OR** urine M protein ≥ 200 mg/24 h **OR** (involved serum free light chain ≥ 10 mg/dL **AND** abnormal serum free‑light‑chain ratio < 0.26 or > 1.65). general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another malignancy within three years (except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, in situ lesions, or cured low‑risk prostate cancer).
Central nervous system involvement.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 3, or grade 2 with painful symptoms.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmia, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, grade 3 thromboembolism, or myocardial infarction within six months.
Daily systemic corticosteroid use exceeding 10 mg prednisone equivalents for longer than seven days.
Recent major surgery or therapeutic radiation within four weeks (limited palliative radiation for bone pain permitted).
Concurrent symptomatic AL amyloidosis or plasma cell leukemia.
POEMS syndrome.
Active infection requiring antimicrobial therapy within fourteen days.
Active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive; inadequate contraception compliance for females of child‑bearing potential; refusal of mandated contraception practices.
Male participants refusing condom use with partners of reproductive potential or refusing agreed‑upon abstinence.
Use of any additional investigational medicinal product concurrently.
Incapacity to receive recommended thromboprophylaxis.
Known hypersensitivity to dexamethasone, sucrose‑histidine, boron, mannitol, polysorbate 80, or any component of the study medication formulation.

general_exclusion_criteria: Participation in other clinical trials with investigational agents within 30 days of start and thereafter. Diagnosis or treatment of another malignancy within 3 years prior to enrolment, except completed resection of basal/squamous cell skin cancer, in situ lesions, or low‑risk prostate cancer cured surgically. Presence of central nervous system involvement. Recent concomitant use of strong cytochrome P450 3A inducers or inhibitors, or strong CYP1A2 inhibitors, within 14 days of first dose. Significant medical or psychiatric illnesses judged to increase risk or impair compliance. Active graft‑versus‑host disease of any severity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Major surgical procedure or radiotherapy within 14 days preceding therapy (limited palliative irradiation beyond 14 days permissible). Uncontrolled cardiovascular disorders—including uncontrolled hypertension, significant arrhythmias, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, stroke, or grade 3 thromboembolic event within six months. Administration of cytotoxic chemotherapies within 21 days (42 days for nitrosoureas). Receipt of live vaccinations within 30 days. Use of immunomodulatory agents or proteasome inhibitors within 14 days. Exposure to other investigational medicines or monoclonal antibodies within 4 weeks. Autologous peripheral stem cell collection/transplant within 12 weeks. Chronic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg prednisone equivalents daily (unless physiologic dosing stabilized ≥7 days) or intermittent glucocorticoids within 14 days. Concomitant symptomatic AL amyloidosis or plasma cell leukemic transformation. POEMS syndrome. Ongoing infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy or serious infection within 7 days of commencement (prophylactic antibiotics alone allowable). Documented active HIV infection, hepatitis B or C infection (active replication confirmed by PCR leads to exclusion). Pregnancy or nursing mothers. Enrollment in any additional investigational therapeutic regimen. Severe hypersensitivity reactions to prior IMiDs or any study drug constituents. Inability to receive appropriate thromboprophylaxis. Insufficient recovery (greater than grade 2 toxicity) from acute adverse effects of antecedent chemotherapy.

Histology allowed: Nodular sclerosis or Mixed cellularity or Lymphocyte‑rich or Lymphocyte‑depleted or Not Otherwise Specified. Cancer burden allowed: Stage I or Stage II disease and at least one bi-dimensionally measurable lesion with longest diameter ≥ 1.5 cm. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or antibody‑based treatment for classic Hodgkin lymphoma; prior solid organ transplant; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with nodular lymphocyte‑predominant Hodgkin lymphoma.
History of active interstitial pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Inherited or acquired severe immunodeficiency requiring ongoing systemic immunosuppression (e.g., primary immunodeficiency syndromes, organ transplant recipients).
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses that threaten safety (significant infections, uncontrolled autoimmune diseases, serious cardiac arrhythmias, unstable angina, significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing medication intake).
Peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1 at enrollment or known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Concurrent malignancies whose course or treatment could confound evaluation of the study regimen.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months before screening.; Presence of pleural effusion requiring therapeutic drainage within twenty‐eight days of screening.; Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy or uncontrolled despite treatment.; Untreated central nervous system (brain) metastases.; Inability or refusal to comply with long‑term safety follow‑up procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 70 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: radiographically confirmed distant/metastatic disease (central nervous system involvement allowable if untreated ≤1 cm lesion not requiring urgent therapy, previously treated lesion not requiring urgent therapy, or clinically stable disease off steroids/EAA medication). Prior treatment required: progression on prior endocrine therapy **or** prior palbociclib **or** prior ribociclib **or** prior chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: ER⁺ and/or PR⁺ by IHC and HER2‑negative by IHC/ISH assessment performed locally. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgery within 14 days before study drug initiation or incomplete recovery; current use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped 7 days prior; receipt of other investigational therapy; additional anticancer agents aside from prescribed study regimens; co‑medications that may affect pharmacokinetics (immune suppressants excluding corticosteroids, systemic cytochrome inhibitors, herbal products); known hypersensitivity to abemaciclib components; active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or detected viral infection (HIV positivity, active Hepatitis B/C); significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled N/V/D, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resection); serious cardiovascular history (syncopal episodes of cardiac origin, pathogenic ventricular arrhythmias, sudden cardiac arrest); uncontrolled/severe comorbidities posing undue safety risk (severe interstitial lung disease, resting hypoxemia, severe renal insufficiency ‑ eGFR < 30 mL/min, hepatic dysfunction exceeding stipulated labs, ongoing steroid dependence for brain metastasis); inability to ingest oral tablets; severe pre‑existent respiratory disease (grade ≥ 2 dyspnoea, oxygen dependency); chronic pancreatic disease; inflammatory bowel disease leading to baseline grade ≥ 2 diarrhoea; lack of adherence/compliance history; second malignant neoplasm diagnosed within two years with active disease (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or cured cervical cancer).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: Morphological complete remission, complete remission with incomplete haematologic recovery or morphologic leucemia‑free state (<5 % blasts) PLUS measurable residual disease detectable by flow cytometry, next‑generation sequencing or polymerase chain reaction (isolates of DNMT3A, TET2 or ASXL1 alone do not satisfy MRD⁺). Prior treatment required: Receiving a hypomethylating agent (azacitidine **or** decitabine) in combination with venetoclax and completion of at least one full cycle of this combination; prior conventional chemotherapy permissible; prior allogeneic stem‑cell transplant allowable only if performed >6 months before enrolment and off chronic immunosuppression. Prior treatment excluded: Allogeneic stem‑cell transplant ≤6 months ago; any solid‑organ transplant; donor lymphocyte infusion; chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy; prior natural‑kill​er cell therapy; concomitant strong or moderate CYP3A inhibitors not dose‑adjusted for venetoclax. Biomarkers required: Presence of ANY ONE of the 2022 ELN adverse‑risk cytogenetic abnormality (**e.g.**, t(6;9), KMT2A rearrangement, t(9;22), inv(3), complex/monosomal karyotype, ‑5/deletion 5q, ‑7, …) **or** any ONE adverse‑risk mutational lesion (mutated TP53, ASXL1, BCOR, EZH2, RUNX1, SF3B1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2) **or** any ONE venetoclax‑resistance alteration (mutated NRAS, KRAS, FLT3‑ITD, FLT3‑TKD). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of grade > 1 non‑haematologic toxicity persisting from prior therapy (with exceptions for low‑grade alopecia or sensory neuropathy).
Active autoimmune gastrointestinal disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease) or systemic autoimmune disorder requiring steroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Uncontrolled hepatitis B or C infection.
Concurrent malignant neoplasm unless in complete remission ≥2 years or meets specific exception criteria (non‑metastatic melanoma, surgically removed cutaneous SCC, non‑metastatic prostate cancer not requiring systemic therapy).
Severe allergy to IL‑2 or structurally related substances.
Use of any other investigational agent for acute myeloid leukaemia.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses (active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmias, untreated central nervous system pathology, major psychiatric disorders limiting adherence).
Grade ≥ 2 haemolytic anaemia documented historically.
Live vaccinations administered within the preceding six months.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: glioma. Histology allowed: oligodendroglioma. general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to swallow oral medication.
Pregnant or breastfeeding.
Participation in another interventional study simultaneously.
Leptomeningeal disease.
Known coagulopathy.
Received prior therapy with an IDH inhibitor, IDH‑directed vaccine, or bevacizumab.
History of another concurrent primary cancer, except curatively resected non‑melanoma skin cancer or cured carcinoma in situ; other past malignancies allowable if disease free >3 years.
Known diagnosis of replication‑repair‑deficient glioma (e.g., constitutional mismatch repair deficiency or Lynch syndrome).
Hypersensitivity to any component or metabolite of vorasidenij or temozolomide.
Significant active cardiac disease within six months (New York Heart Association class III or IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke).
Corrected QT interval ≥450 ms or other risk factors for QT prolongation/arrhythmias.
Active hepatitis B or C infection, or untreated HIV infection/AIDs‑related illness (controlled/chronic infection may be permitted per protocol).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of interstitial lung disease or clinically active pulmonary fibrosis/pneumonitis.
Congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association class III or IV, or severe cardiac arrhythmias requiring treatment.
Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) greater than 470 ms.
Use of concomitant medicines that markedly prolong QT/QTc interval (unless cleared by investigator).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Other primary malignancy diagnosed within the past 2 years, except adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ.
Malabsorptive syndromes or gastrointestinal surgeries impairing oral drug absorption.
Uncontrolled comorbidities: acute or worsening cardiovascular, respiratory, hepatic, or renal disease; active infection including untreated HIV, active tuberculosis, hepatitis B core positivity with detectable HBV DNA, or active hepatitis C with detectable RNA.
For persons living with HIV/AIDS: CD4⁺ count ≥350 cells/mm³, no opportunistic infection in preceding 12 months, on stable antiretroviral therapy ≥4 weeks with HIV viral load <400 copies/ml.
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers, P-gp modulators, or CYP3A4-sensitive substrates that cannot be discontinued or substituted.
Active bleeding disorder.
Previous solid organ transplantation.
Any condition judged by the investigator to place the participant at undue risk or impede proper assessment of study drug safety.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma of the breast. Cancer burden allowed: Pathological stage IA (clinical stage I) invasive carcinoma, tumor ≤2 cm, node‐negative (pN0) or isolated tumor cells / micrometastasis (pN1mic), no evidence of distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: Complete surgical resection of the index tumor with negative margins together with sentinel lymph node biopsy or axillary dissection documenting N0 or N1mic status; surgery must have occurred ≤90 days before intended start of protocol therapy; optional receipt of up to 4 weeks of adjuvant endocrine therapy prior to randomisation; optional completion of partial‑breast radiation (≤14 days total) finished ≥2 weeks before protocol initiation. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic chemotherapy delivered within the prior 5 years—including paclitaxel—and any whole‑breast external beam radiation therapy administered before enrolment. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive status verified by ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines and confirmed by centralized testing; documented estrogen‑receptor and progesterone‑receptor status (results may be positive or negative); provision of archived tumor tissue sufficient for correlative research. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy; breastfeeding; women of childbearing potential or men with partners of childbearing potential who refuse mandated non‑hormonal contraception; locally advanced breast tumors at presentation (e.g., fixation to chest wall, peau d’ orange, extensive skin invasion, inflammatory change); personal history of prior invasive breast cancer; prior systemic chemotherapy within the past five years; prior exposure to paclitaxel; active viral hepatitis (B or C) or other clinically significant liver disease; severe concomitant illness such as untreated serious infection, end‑stage renal disease requiring dialysis, unstable cardiovascular disease, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, recent myocardial infarction with residual symptoms, significant psychiatric disorders impairing adherence, or any condition judged by the investigators to compromise safety or protocol compliance.

Prior treatment required: infusion of ≥ one investigational cryopreserved cord blood unit manufactured by the National Cord Blood Program. Prior treatment excluded: licensed cord blood product only or unlicensed cord blood product from other banks only or cord blood product that has been manipulated post‑thaw (e.g., ex vivo expansion, incubation in vitro). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic reaction to blood components, active infection requiring systemic antibiotics, uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (heart failure NYHA class III–IV), significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 2× ULN or AST/ALT > 5× ULN), clinically meaningful renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min), unresolved grade ≥2 pulmonary toxicity/pneumonitis, untreated central nervous system involvement or symptomatic brain metastasis, known human immunodeficiency virus infection, chronic viral hepatitis B or C with detectable viremia, pregnancy or breastfeeding, Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≥ 3.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma. Histology allowed: Glioblastoma WHO grade IV. general_exclusion_criteria:
Known resistant mutations in tumor tissue or liquid biopsy (e.g., EGFR T790M, MET).  
History of interstitial lung disease associated with EGFR TKI usage.  
Symptomatic or radiographically evident leptomeningeal disease.  
Symptomatic brain metastases or spinal cord compression necessitating urgent medical intervention.  
Unresolved toxicity attributable to prior therapy.  
Significant cardiovascular disease.  
Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks before study entry or anticipated during participation.  
Ongoing or recent anticancer therapy or radiation therapy.  
Presence of another malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment within the ensuing 24 months.  
Active hepatitis B or C infection and/or known Human Immunodeficiency Virus positivity.  
Poorly controlled gastrointestinal disorders.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma (associated with Waldenström macroglobulinemia). Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease defined as serum IgM greater than twice the upper limit of normal together with symptomatic disease meeting consensus indication criteria (one or more constitutional symptom, recurrent fever, night sweats, fatigue, weight loss, progressive or symptomatic lymphadenopathy or splenomegaly, hemoglobin <=10 g/dL, platelets <=100 k/µL, hyperviscosity syndrome, symptomatic peripheral neuropathy, systemic amyloidosis, renal insufficiency, symptomatic cryoglobulinemia). Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of systemic therapy for Waldenström macroglobulinemia (previous covalent Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor allowable; previous venetoclax allowable provided no documented disease progression on venetoclax). Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to non‑covalent Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors OR documented disease progression while on prior venetoclax OR occurrence of a major bleeding event or grade >3 arrhythmia attributable to a prior BTK inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Baseline serum IgM >= 2 times upper limit of normal. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants currently receiving any other investigational agents.; Pregnant, nursing, or women intending to become pregnant during study or within six months after last dose.; Individuals with known central nervous system lymphoma.; Persons with confirmed human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Active hepatitis B infection (positive HB sAg) or hepatitis B DNA positivity; Active hepatitis C infection (positive HCV RNA).; Ongoing use of warfarin.; Concomitant use of moderate or strong CYP3A4 modulators or strong P‑glycoprotein inhibitors.; Current systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding low-dose corticosteroids (<20 mg/day prednisone equivalent).; Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within four weeks preceding first study dose.; Untreated acute severe bacterial, viral, fungal or parasitic infection (excluding superficial fungal nail infection).; Major surgical procedure within four weeks before starting study medication.; Conditions preventing oral intake of tablets.; Documented history of alcohol or illicit drug dependence interfering with compliance.; Inability to swallow capsules or adhere to scheduled outpatient follow‑up.; Uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenias requiring escalation of therapy within four weeks prior to enrolment.; Corrected QT interval (>470 ms) observed on serial electrocardiograms during screening.; Clinically significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, myocardial infarction within past six months, left ventricular ejection fraction <=45 %, NYHA class III-IV heart failure, or untreated symptomatic arrhythmias.; Any serious coexisting medical disorder judged by investigators to jeopardize patient safety or data integrity.; Hypersensitivity to components of pirtobrutinib or venetoclax formulations.; History of persistent non‑adherence to prescribed therapeutic regimens.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with uncontrolled seizures.
Presence of a central nervous system tumor, CNS metastasis, CNS ischemia, or cerebral infarction regardless of symptomatology.
Baseline corrected QTc greater than 480 ms.
Inability to return for scheduled follow‑up visits or complete required assessments.
Active untreated infections.
Requirement for systemic corticosteroids exceeding 0.15 mg/kg/day prednisolone equivalent (unless inhaled/topical/adrenal replacement dosing).
Use of additional immunosuppressive medication intended to modulate immune-related adverse events (excluding low-dose inhaled/topical forms).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of myocardial infarction, unstable angina, severe peripheral vascular disease, or clinically significant arrhythmias within the preceding year.
Presence of any immunodeficiency disorder, including documented Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
Evidence of chronic active hepatitis B or C infection (positive HBsAg with detectable HBV DNA, or anti‑HBc IgM/IgG with detectable HCV RNA).
Active uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or opportunistic infection.
Significant concomitant medical conditions judged to markedly increase risk (severe cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, serious pulmonary disorders, etc.).
Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inducers initiated within two weeks prior to study drug administration.
Recent receipt of local radiotherapy to a sole RECIST target lesion where confirmation of progression cannot be established.
Documented myopathy (except remote, fully resolved steroid‑induced cases).
Any second malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the past three years, except for tumors with very low mortality risk (e.g., appropriately treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, cured localized prostate cancer, non‑muscle invasive bladder cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, Stage I endometrial carcinoma).
Women who are pregnant or breastfeeding, and sexually active individuals not employing adequate contraception throughout the study period and for the stipulated post‑treatment interval.
Clinical situations requiring immediate tumor reduction due to imminent compromise of vital structures.

Active inflammatory or infective ophthalmic conditions, or a history of idiopathic or auto­immune‐associated uveitis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals.
* Individuals unable or unwilling to use highly effective dual-method contraception throughout the study.
* Current systemic corticosteroid use without a stable/decreasing regimen for ≥7 days before enrolment.
* Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study.
* Receipt of any other antitumour therapeutic agents concurrently.
* Use of calcineurin inhibitors (cyclosporine, tacrolimus) or other graft‑versus‑host disease prophylactic medications.
* Concomitant strong inducers or inhibitors of CYP3A4 (must be stopped ≥14 days before first dose).
* Bisphosphonate therapy within 28 days preceding enrolment.
* Denosumab receipt within 180 days preceding enrolment.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or signs suggesting evolving MDS/AML.
QTcF greater than 470 ms on screening electrocardiogram.
Allergic reaction attributable to axatilimab, olaparib, or structurally similar compounds.
Concurrent use of strong or moderate cytochrome P450 (especially CYP3A) inhibitors or inducers.
Inability to safely ingest oral medication or presence of gastrointestinal disorder expected to impair drug absorption (e.g., severe malabsorptive syndromes, extensive gastric or intestinal resection).
Uncontrolled comorbidities such as poorly managed hypertension, active serious infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or untreated/unstable psychiatric condition limiting adherence.
Past or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease or radiographic evidence thereof at baseline.
Active or latent Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Major surgical operation within 28 days preceding first dose of study therapy.

HIV infection not suppressed (detectable VL), active hepatitis B or C infections not adequately treated/suppressed.

History of significant cardiac dysfunction (shortening fraction <27%, ejection fraction <50%) despite being addressed in inclusion laboratory limits.

Uncontrolled central nervous system involvement (active untreated CNS disease).
Active infection with HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Systemic light chain amyloidosis. Histology allowed: Amyloid deposits showing Congo red positivity with apple‑green birefringence plus identifiable clonal plasma‑cell production of light chains. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after at least one prior anti‑plasma‑cell therapy; cardiac involvement staged as Mayo/Cardiac Risk stage I, II, or IIIa (NYHA class I–II, NT‑proBNP < 8500 pg/mL, Troponin I/T < threshold); bone‑marrow plasma‑cell infiltrates ≤60% with no osteolytic lesions; measurable disease defined by serum M‑spike ≥500 mg/dL or serum free light chain ≥20 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio or differential free light chain ≥20 mg/L. Prior treatment required: Minimum one prior line of anti‑myeloma / plasma‑cell directed therapy (any regimen). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior exposure to ixazomib (MLN9708) or venetoclax. Biomarkers required: Presence of chromosome translocation t(11;14) demonstrated by FISH on bone‑marrow sample. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with untreated or uncontrolled central nervous system involvement.
History of hypersensitivity reaction to venetoclax, ixazomib, dexamethasone, or structurally related substances.
Concurrent participation in another investigational therapeutic trial.
Active uncontrolled infectious diseases, including unresolved bacterial, fungal, or parasitic infections.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions such as symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction (within six months), clinically significant arrhythmias, or NYHA class III/IV symptoms unrelated to approved cardiac staging limits.
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN or AST/ALT >3× ULN) despite being addressed elsewhere in inclusion criteria.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <15 mL/min) not covered by standard laboratory allowances.
Pregnant or lactating individuals; females of childbearing potential unwilling/unable to comply with mandated contraception measures.
Psychiatric or social circumstances compromising adherence to study procedures.
Any condition judged by the investigator to jeopardize participant safety or compromise interpretation of results.

general_exclusion_criteria: Known hypersensitivity or contraindication to components of REM‑422 or related compounds. Currently active hepatitis B or C viral infection. Primary immunodeficiency disorders. Requirement for daily systemic glucocorticoids ≥ 10 mg prednisolone equivalents (topical or low‑dose inhaled steroids permissible). Receipt of live vaccination ≤ 6 weeks before first dose of REM‑422. Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers. Recent use (within 7 days) of gastric pH–altering agents such as H₂ blockers or proton‑pump inhibitors. Pregnant individuals, persons intending conception during the study, or breastfeeding women. Malabsorptive syndromes, severe gastrointestinal functional impairment, or gastrectomy/bowel resections compromising drug uptake. Use of prohibited concomitant medicines within 1 week preceding study start. Significant underlying cardiac disease. Major surgery involving breach of a mesenchymal barrier (pleura, peritoneum, meninges) or surgeries needing general anaesthesia performed < 4 weeks before enrolment. History of solid-organ transplantation necessitating ongoing immunosuppression. Ongoing or historical autoimmune diseases (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease, rheumatologic conditions, systemic lupus erythematosus). Received therapeutic radiation ≤ 7 days before initiating REM‑422. Participation in another interventional investigational product study ≤ 3 weeks before entry. general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unable to ingest orally or absorb medication; individuals incapable of providing informed consent; persons with significant suicide risk (suicidal ideation with intent/plans within past six months or at screening); anyone with a diagnosed primary psychotic disorder, major depressive episode with psychosis, bipolar I disorder, dissociative identity disorder, or currently active substance use disorder within the past year; subjects taking prohibited concomitant drugs that pose irreversible drug–drug interaction risk despite permissible tapering (including serotininergic antidepressants, SNRIs, TCAs, efavirenz, serotonergic supplements, MAO inhibitors, antipsychotics, mood stabilisers, aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitors, strong UGT1A0/A1A10 inhibitors); individuals with clinically significant uncontrolled haemato­logical, endocrinologic, cerebrovascular, cardiovascular, coronary, pulmonary, renal, gastrointestinal, immune-compromised, or neurologic illnesses (except well-controlled hypothyroidism on stable replacement); patients with untreated brain tumour or brain metastases; diagnoses of cirrhosis or hepatic failure; uncontrolled hypertension (≥140/90 mm Hg confirmed on three readings); resting heart rate >100 beats/minute recorded on three occasions; histories of ventricular arrhythmia (other than isolated premature ventricular complexes absent ischaemic heart disease), Wolff‑Parkinson‑White syndrome or unablated accessory pathways, recent (>12 months) symptomatic arrhythmias (atrial fibrillation/flutter/tachyarrhythmias) unless adequately treated, risk factors predisposing to torsades de pointe (heart failure, hypokalaemia, familial prolonged QT); prior myocardial infarction, coronary artery disease, or congestive heart failure; markedly prolonged baseline QTc interval (>450 ms in males, >460 ms in females, adjusted for hormone therapy duration where applicable); pregnant or breastfeeding women, or women capable of conception not employing effective contraception; hypersensitivity to any component of the investigational medicinal product.

* History of Class III or IV congestive heart failure, severe non‑ischemic cardiomyopathy, recent (<6 months) unstable angina, myocardial infarction, or ventricular arrhythmia.
* Past/intercurrent interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced ILD, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids, or any clinically active ILD.
* Symptomatic Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
* Active infection requiring treatment or unresolved Hepatitis B/C infection (patients positive for HBsAg with normal HBV DNA permitted).
* Active gastrointestinal disorders affecting absorption (e.g., Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, short bowel syndrome) or other malabsorptive syndromes.
* Uncontrolled concomitant serious medical, psychiatric, or substance abuse conditions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Acute Myeloid Leukaemia. Histology allowed: Acute Myeloid Leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: First remission after high‑intensity chemotherapy **or** at least two cycles of low‑intensity therapy (hypomethylating agent or low‑dose cytarabine) with bone‑marrow blasts < 5 % and measurable residual disease ≥ 0.1 % by centrally performed multi‑parameter flow cytometry; or second remission after any therapy satisfying the same morphological and MRD thresholds; no clinically active extramedullary disease; no active central nervous system leukaemia. Prior treatment required: Induction/high‑intensity chemotherapy leading to first remission **or** ≥2 cycles of low‑intensity therapy (hypomethylating agent or low‑dose cytarabine) achieving remission; alternative any prior therapy yielding a second remission. Prior treatment excluded: Prior menin inhibitor therapy. Biomarkers required: NPM1 mutation **or** KMT2A (MLL) rearrangement **or** NUP98 rearrangement. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Active uncontrolled infection.
Recent (within 6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class ≥ II, life‑threatening arrhythmia, cerebrovascular accident, or transient ischemic attack.
QTc prolongation greater than 450 ms in males or 470 ms in females (Fridericia formula).
Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorders interfering with oral medication absorption.
Concurrent active malignant neoplasm undergoing treatment.
Requirement for imminent hematopoietic stem cell transplantation within 30 days.
Use of additional antileukemic agents during the study aside from permissible intrathecal chemotherapy, hydroxyurea for rapid proliferation/count control, or corticosteroids for differentiation syndrome.
Ongoing systemic immunosuppressive therapy post–allogeneic transplant without requisite washouts (off systemic immunosuppression ≥2 weeks, calcineurin inhibitors ≥4 weeks).
Grade > 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease, moderate-to-severe limited chronic graft‑vs‑host disease, or extensive chronic graft‑vs‑host disease.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of seizure or any neurologic condition predisposing to seizure (e.g., recent stroke, cerebral vascular malformations, intracranial neoplasms).
Recent serious cardiovascular events: severe or unstable angina, myocardial infarction, arterial or venous thromboembolism, or clinically significant ventricular arrhythmia occurring within 12 months.
New York Heart Association functional class III or IV heart failure.
Active uncontrolled infection.
Uncontrolled hypertension (persistently systolic ≥160 mmHg or diastolic ≥100 mmHg despite antihypertensive therapy).
Need for chronic systemic corticosteroids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent.
Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh class B or C).
Inability to swallow oral capsules/tablets.
Participation in another therapeutic clinical trial concurrently.
Presence of inflammatory bowel disease.

general_exclusion_criteria: History or current diagnosis of impaired cardiac function, including recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, cardiomyopathy, symptomatic pericarditis, or coronary artery bypass graft surgery; long QT syndrome, familial sudden cardiac death syndromes, baseline resting QTcF ≥450 ms (male) or ≥460 ms (female), electrolytes predisposing to torsades de pointes (unadjusted hypo­kalemia, hypocalcaemia, hypomagnesemia), concomitant drugs that significantly prolong QT when discontinuation is impossible; clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias (ventricular tachyarrhythmia, high‑grade atrioventricular block), left ventricular ejection fraction <50%; uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic ≥160 mm Hg or diastolic ≥100 mm Hg); history of another active malignant neoplasm within the preceding three years (excluding adequately treated superficial urothelial carcinoma, carcinoma in situ, or basal/squamous cell skin cancer); severe hematologic impairment (absolute neutrophil count <1.5 × 10⁹/L, platelet count <100 × 10⁹/L, haemoglobin <10 g/dL); moderate-to-severe hepatic dysfunction (alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >3 times upper normal limit, total bilirubin >1.5 times upper normal limit unless due to Gilberts’ syndrome under stipulated thresholds); reduced renal function (creatinine clearance <60 ml/min); chronic pancreatic insufficiency leading to persistent lipase elevations deemed clinically significant; any active infection such as untreated HIV, hepatitis B/C, or other uncontrolled infectious diseases; pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; ECOG/Karnofsky performance status indicative of unacceptable functional limitation (generally >2).

Prior treatment required: Completion of external beam radiation therapy for glioblastoma (≥12 weeks before study initiation).
