Biomarkers required: Positive ^68Ga‑Ga‑NeoB uptake in the tumour region observed on PET/CT or PET/MRI. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Acute or uncontrolled medical illness or history of allogeneic organ transplant.  
• Mixed small cell lung cancer and non‑small cell lung cancer histology.  
• Major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding first dose of study drug.  
• Active or prior autoimmune or inflammatory disorder requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy (excluding isolated vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, dietary‑only celiac disease, or chronic skin conditions not needing systemic therapy).  
• Medical contraindication to platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy.  
•• History of another primary malignancy unless treated with curative intent and free of disease ≥2 years (or adequately resected non‑melanoma skin cancer or in‑situ disease).  
• Persistent grade ≥2 adverse events attributable to prior anticancer therapy (unless deemed safe by investigator).  
• Symptomatic or uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis, leptomeningeal carcinomatosis, or newly diagnosed brain metastases not stabilized according to protocol specifications.  
• Positive tests for active Mycobacterium tuberculosis, hepatitis B surface antigen, hepatitis C viral replication, or uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.  
• Clinically significant cardiac disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, or myocardial infarction within the prior six months.  
• Concomitant receipt of any other investigational agent, biological product, or systemic anticancer therapy (hormonal therapy for non‑oncologic indications permitted).  
• Radiotherapy exceeding protocol allowances (outside of approved definitive (>6 months ago) or limited palliative settings).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or progressive intracranial disease measurable by RANO 2.0, without diffuse leptomeningeal disease or extracranial disease. Prior treatment required: prior cranial radiotherapy with or without chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to troriluzole or riluzole. Biomarkers required: IDH1/IDH2 wildtype confirmed by DNA sequencing in a CLIA/CAP‑certified laboratory. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count < 1500/mm³; platelets < 100,000/mm³; hemoglobin < 9 g/dL; AST or ALT > 1.5×upper limit of normal; total bilirubin > 2×ULN (except Gilberts’ syndrome allowance); serum creatinine > 1.5 mg/dL or creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min.  
Positive serum ß‑hCG in individuals assigned female at birth of child‑bearing potential.  
History of interstitial lung disease.  
Active or untreated hepatitis B, hepatitis C requiring antivirals, or HIV infection.  
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.  
Significant cardiovascular disease or NYHA functional classification worse than Class IIb.  
Uncontrolled infectious diseases needing systemic therapy.  
Recent major surgery (within 28 days).  
Contraindications to magnetic resonance imaging.  
Use of concomitant strong CYP1A2 inhibitors/induces or hepatotoxic drugs lacking sufficient washout.  
Any medically serious, acute, or chronic condition judged by investigator to compromise safety, protocol adherence, or data integrity.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of another invasive malignancy unless definitively treated with curative intent and disease free for ≥5 years (exceptions detailed in protocol). Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (e.g., New York Heart Association class II–IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, uncontrolled arrhythmia). Severe pulmonary disorders including prior radiation pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Baseline laboratory abnormalities indicative of severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5 times ULN not attributed to Gilbert’s syndrome), renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min), or profound myelosuppression despite supportive care. Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic hepatitis B/C infection with detectable viral load. Pregnancy or lactation, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception. Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 3 to <30 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: central nervous system germinoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
* Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals.
* Individuals unable or unwilling to use adequate contraception throughout the study.
* Performance status worse than ECOG 2/Karnofsky <60%.
* Active uncontrolled cardiac insufficiency or recent myocardial infarction.
* Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >135 U/L).
* Severe renal dysfunction exceeding protocol-specified laboratory cutoffs.
* Ongoing clinically significant pulmonary disease, interstitial pneumonia, or history of Grade ≥ 2 pneumonitis.
* Untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system hemorrhage or symptomatic brain edema.
* Current untreated opportunistic infections, including uncontrolled HIV/AIDS, chronic Hepatitis B/C with detectable viremia.
* Concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy.
* Known hypersensitivity to carboplatin, etoposide, or related agents.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Recent major surgical procedure performed within a few weeks preceding study entry.
* Recent systemic anticancer therapy administered shortly before study initiation.
* Palliative thoracic radiation delivered within three months prior to study entry or radiation to other anatomical sites within two weeks prior to study entry.
* Presence of an additional malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment within two years prior to screening, excluding adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer, or carcinoma in situ of the breast or cervix.
* Untreated central nervous system metastases, newly diagnosed or progressing brain metastases, history of leptomeningeal spread or carcinomatous meningitis.
* Significant underlying liver disease such as cirrhosis, hepatic fibrosis, esophageal or gastric varices, or other clinically meaningful hepatic impairment.
* Uncontrolled systemic illnesses—including clinically significant cardiovascular, pulmonary, or metabolic disorders—that could elevate risk of serious adverse events or impede protocol assessments.
* Clinically significant preexisting cardiovascular disease.

Patients with pathologic fracture or impending fracture at the index lesion or scheduled for surgical fixation of that bone.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 3 to 75 years. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Acquired severe aplastic anemia. Histology allowed: Hypocellular bone marrow (cellularity <25%). Cancer burden allowed: Severe aplasia demonstrated by bone marrow hypoplasia together with at least two of the following peripheral criteria: absolute neutrophil count <0.5 ×10⁹/L, platelet count <20 ×10⁹/L, reticulocyte count <20 ×10⁹/L. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Anti‑thymocyte globulin, calcineurin inhibitors, thrombopoietin‐receptor agonists, androgens used for treating severe aplastic anemia; any prior allogeneic stem cell transplant; any prior solid organ transplant; any prior desensitisation procedure directed at reducing donor‑specific HLA antibodies (e.g., plasma exchange, ivIg, MMF); prior systemic anticancer therapy (excluding remotely cured basal cell carcinoma or treated cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia). Biomarkers required: Absence of donor‑specific HLA antibodies with mean fluorescence intensity >3000 (screen negative); lack of clonal cytogenetic lesions characteristic of myelodysplastic syndrome (e.g., monosomy 7); demonstration of acquired—not inherited—bone‑marrow failure (negative Fanconi anaemia chromosome fragility test or normal telomere length). Biomarkers excluded: Detectable donor‑specific HLA antibodies (MFI >3000); clonal cytogenetic abnormalities defining myelodysplastic syndrome; pathogenic variants associated with inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Fanconi anaemia genes, short‑telomere syndrome genes); uncontrolled HIV viremia; detectable replicating hepatitis B or hepatitis C viruses.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant congestive heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction ≤40%.
Significant hepatic dysfunction (total bilirubin ≥2.0 mg/dL absentGilbert’s disease).
Renal impairment (estimated CrCl ≤60 mL/min for persons ≥13 y; pediatric GFR <90 mL/min/1.73 m² confirmed by formal measurement if <90).
Inadequate pulmonary function (DLco ≤50% predicted, or FEV₁/FVC ≤50%; or resting SaO₂ ≤92% on ambient air).
Performance status Karnofsky/Lansky score <60%.
Pregnancy or current lactation.
Uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection despite appropriate antimicrobial therapy.
Life‑threatening hypersensitivity/allergic reaction to rabbit‑derived antithymocyte globulin (Thymoglobulin®).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: Richter's transformation (chronic lymphocytic leukemia/small lymphocytic lymphoma transformed to diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma). Histology allowed: diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma arising from CLL/SLL. Cancer burden allowed: biopsy‑proven transformation (fresh or archival specimen accepted). general_exclusion_criteria:
* History of Hodgkin‑type variant transformation of CLL.  
* Prior anti‑CD20 bispecific antibody therapy.  
* Recent (<14 days) receipt of investigational agents, targeted therapies, immune‑stimulating agents, checkpoint blockers, radiolabeled antibodies, ADCs (except noted exceptions).  
* Prior CAR‑T cell therapy within 30 days.  
* Persistent >Grade 1 toxicity from preceding anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia, endocrine deficiency on replacement, stable vitiligo).  
* Bulky cervical adenopathy causing potential airway obstruction.  
* Other concurrent malignant neoplasms except cured CLL/SLL, adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, low‑risk prostate cancer on active surveillance.  
* Ongoing Grade >1 peripheral neuropathy (polatuzumab cohort).  
* Immune‑related Grade ≥3 adverse events (unless endocrine disorder on replacement).  
* Confirmed progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.  
* Central nervous system involvement or leptomeningeal disease; historic cerebrovascular accident, seizure disorder, vasculitis, neurodegeneration (stroke/TIA < 2 years permissible if resolved).  
* Interstitial lung disease histories (idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, active pneumonitis on imaging).  
* Recurrent uncontrollable pleural, pericardial, or abdominal effusions needing frequent drainage.  
* Solid organ transplantation.  
* Suspected or known haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.  
* Active or poorly controlled autoimmune disease (myocarditis, pneumonitis, Myasthenia gravis, myositis, AI hepatitis, SLE, RA, IBD, APS, GPA/Wegener’s, Sjögren’s, GBM, MS, vasculitis, GN); well‑controlled cutaneous-only disease allowable.  
* Autoimmune cytopaenias requiring escalation of therapy within 4 weeks (specific considerations for pirtobrutinib arm).  
* Systemic immunosuppressants (prednisone, azathioprine, methotrexate, thalidomide, TNF antagonists) within 2 weeks of cycle 1 day 1 (exceptions for inhaled steroids, mineralocorticoids, steroid support for lymphoma symptoms).  
* Severe IgE‑mediated reaction to monoclonal antibodies unless desensitisation feasible.  
* Major surgery or significant trauma within 4 weeks or incomplete postoperative recovery.  
* Positive HIV infection (excludes pirtobrutinib arm; elsewhere requires CD4 ≥ 200, suppressed VL).  
* HTLV‑1 positivity.  
* Active CMV infection.  
* Clinically significant liver disease (cirrhosis, active hepatitis); HBV core Ag or HBsAg positive must have undetectable DNA and accept antiviral prophylaxis; HCV RNA must be negative.  
* Acute infection necessitating inpatient care or intravenous antimicrobials within 4 weeks; however prophylactic antibiotics/antivirals/fungi permitted.  
* Live vaccine administration within 28 days prior to start; avoidance of live vaccines throughout study (inactive flu OK).  
* Cardiovascular events within 6 months (MI, unstable angina, CABG/PTCA, congestive HF NYHA III‑IV, LVEF ≤ 40%, uncontrolled arrhythmia, prolonged QTc > 470 msec). Pacemaker carriers allowed if asymptomatic.  
* Significant GI malabsorption affecting drug uptake.  
* Requirement for therapeutic Vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation.  
* Hypersensitivity to study drug components.  
* Previous major bleed or Grade ≥ 3 arterial thrombotic event on BTK inhibition.  
* Bleeding diatheses (von Willebrand disease, hemophilia).  
* Stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months.  
* Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study participation.  
* inability to abide by mandatory hospitalisations/risk‑mitigation measures.

general_exclusion_criteria: Allergic reactions attributable to compounds similar to durvalumab or tremelimumb.; Presence of an allogeneic organ transplant.; Concurrent participation in another investigational study.; Active or historical autoimmune or inflammatory disorders (exceptions applied separately).; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection (e.g., tuberculosis), uncontrolled hypertension (>140/90 mmHg), interstitial lung disease, severe chronic gastrointestinal disease causing diarrhea, or unmanaged psychiatric/social circumstances.; Primary central nervous system neoplasm (other than benign meningioma), existing brain metastasis, leptomeningeal disease, uncontrolled seizures, or cerebrovascular accident within the preceding year.; Documented primary immunodeficiency.; Positive HIV infection (even though treated individuals may enroll under strict virologic control).; Active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg).; Active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).; Significant cardiac disease such as myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 12 months, New York Heart Association class II or higher heart failure, uncontrolled ventricular or rapid atrial arrhythmias, or substantial peripheral/vacular artery disease.; Clinically evident coagulation disorder, bleeding tendency, or thrombotic episode within 12 months.; Ongoing non‑healing wound, ulcer, bone fracture, or history of pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.; Pregnant or lactating persons.; Dependence on total parenteral nutrition containing lipids.

general_exclusion_criteria: Severe grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab or its excipients; administration of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose of Second Course Phase; diagnosed immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalents) or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to Cycle 1 Day 1; presence of a concomitant actively progressing malignancy or one requiring active treatment (exceptions: cured early-stage cancers, non‑ulcerated thin melanomas, basal/squamous cell skin cancers, in‑situ cervix or breast cancers); active central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease; active autoimmune disease necessitating systemic therapy within the preceding 2 years (physiological hormone replacements exempt); history of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or existing pneumonitis; for non‑small cell lung cancer participants specifically, documented interstitial lung disease; active infectious process demanding systemic antimicrobial therapy; confirmed HIV infection; chronic hepatitis B or C infection (eligibility follows parent-study rules when applicable); pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or cause conception during study participation and until 120 days post‑last dose; severe cardiovascular disorders—including class III/IV heart failure, symptomatic ischemic heart disease, significant arrhythmias, or recent (<12 months) acute coronary syndrome, stroke, or unstable angina; hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh score > 6 (Class B/C); uncontrolled thyroid disorder; unmanaged diabetes mellitus; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation; active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection; for participants assigned to receive lenvatinib additionally: major surgery ≤ 3 weeks before first dose, grade ≥ 3 gastrointestinal/non‑gastrointestinal fistulas, urinary protein excretion ≥ 1 g/24 h, left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limit of normal, radiologically evident involvement of major vessels or intra‑tumoral cavitation, prolonged corrected QT interval (> 480 ms), clinically significant cardiovascular events within 12 months, conditions impairing oral drug absorption, active hemoptysis occurring ≤ 3 weeks before initiation, and any known contraindications or severe hypersensitivity to lenvatinib.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Triple negative breast cancer. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of any estrogen‐receptor‑positive or progesterone‑receptor‑positive phenotype; Activating alterations in the phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase pathway (loss-of-function PTEN or PIK3R1, amplification or activating mutation of PIK3CA, AKT, or MTOR).
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Residual toxicity greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
• Receipt of any other investigational agents concurrently.
• History of hormone‑receptor‑positive breast cancer.
• Presence of activating phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase pathway genomic alterations (see biomarkers excluded).
• Prior therapy with BET, RAF, MEK, or ERK inhibitors.
• Allergic reaction to components chemically related to ZEN003694 or binimetinib.
• Requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation (low‑dose prophylaxis permitted).
• Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers (unless stopped ≥ 7 days before starting study drug).
• Current use of medicines that significantly prolong the QT interval.
• Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses.
• Psychiatric disorder or social situation compromising adherence to study protocols.
• Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures.
• Cardiovascular events (stroke, myocardial infarction, unstable angina) within six months preceding enrolment.
• Conditions interfering with oral drug absorption (gastrectomy, intestinal bypass, severe diarrhoea, gastroparesis).
• History of retinal vein occlusion.
• Past episode of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any malignant neoplasm for which an approved epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor is indicated. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Acneiform or papulopustular rash associated with epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor therapy graded ≤ 3 by CTCAE and ARIGA with ≤ 30 % body‑surface‑area involvement; predicted life expectancy ≥ 3 months; ECOG performance status 0–2. Prior treatment required: Current administration of an approved epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor for cancer. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent use of pimozide **or** potent CYP3A4 inhibitors (diltiazem, ketoconazole, itraconazole, nefazodone, troleandomycin, clarithromycin, ritonavir, nelfinavir) **or** potent CYP3A4 inducers (rifampin, carbamazepine, phenytoin). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patient has severe cutaneous toxicity (grade 4 or >30 % BSA involvement) attributed to EGFR inhibitor therapy.
Significant underlying physical or psychological condition making protocol compliance unlikely.
History of chronic skin disorders (atopic dermatitis, psoriasis, recurrent skin infections) that could confound outcomes.
Baseline laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count <1000/mm³, white blood cell count <3000/mm³, platelet count <50,000/mm³, AST >2.5 × upper limit of normal, ALT >2.5 × ULN, bilirubin >1.5 × ULN, creatinine >1.5 × ULN.
Requirement for radiation therapy to head, neck, or upper trunk concurrent with EGFR inhibitor or radiation administered within 4 weeks preceding screening.
Use of aprepitant or other neurokinin‑1 receptor antagonists within 4 weeks prior to screening.
Participation in another investigational drug study within 4 weeks or five half‑lives of the most recently administered investigational agent.
Active infection (e.g., pneumonia) or any uncontrolled disease apart from the target malignancy posing undue risk.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant during the study period.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: At least 1 prior line of systemic therapy in the advanced/metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Previous receipt of neratinib or DS‑8201a. Biomarkers required: HER2 positivity demonstrated by ANY ONE OF the following—(a) Immunohistochemistry score 3⁺; (b) HER2 amplification by ISH (ratio ≥ 2 or average copy number ≥ 6 signals per cell); or (c) Presence of a qualifying HER2 activating mutation (listed in protocol). Assessment of HER2 status must be performed on a CLIA‑certified laboratory specimen obtained before enrolment. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients must not receive other anticancer therapy—including small‑molecule targeted agents, conventional chemotherapies, immune checkpoint inhibitors, or other antibody–drug conjugates—outside of stipulated washout periods. Concurrent investigational agents are prohibited. Major surgical procedure or radiation therapy within four weeks (two weeks for palliative stereotactic irradiation) excludes participation. Thoracic radiotherapy (excluding palliation of metastatic lesions) is disallowed. Chronic systemic glucocorticoids or other immunosuppressants (aside from supportive measures) are forbidden. Chloroquine or hydroxyhydrocquinoline use is prohibited. Live, attenuated vaccinations within thirty days prior to first dose are excluded. History of interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis requiring steroids, existing ILD/pneumonitis, or unexplained pulmonary infiltrates leads to exclusion. Severe underlying pulmonary diseases (recent PE, severe COPD/asthma, restrictive lung disease, extensive pleural effusion, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with lung involvement, prior pneumonectomy) are exclusionary. Allergic reaction to components of DS‑8201a or neratinib, or severe hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies, eliminates eligibility. Strong/moderate CYP3A4 or P‑gp enzyme modulators contraindicated. Recent (< six months) myocardial infarction or symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA Class II–IV excludes participants. Baseline corrected QTc > 470 ms (female) or > 450 ms (male) is prohibitive. Significant corneal pathology, draining pleural/pericardial/ascitic fluid or recent CART (> 2 weeks), spinal cord compression, untreated active infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobials, unresolved Grade ≥ 2 toxicities from prior therapy (unless medically justified), substantial psychiatric/substance‑abuse disorders, pregnancy or lactation, inability to ingest oral medication, ongoing spermatogenesis/sperm donation restrictions for males, oocyte retrieval/fertility preservation activities for females, and prior allogeneic organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplant constitute exclusion criteria.
